blocks:
Sūrat Yūnus

Meccan, and it is 109 verses

Sūrat Yūnus

It is Meccan according to the saying of most scholars, and it is narrated from Ibn Abbas and Qatadah, except for three verses which were revealed in Medina: فَإِنْ كُنْتَ فِي شَكٍ ** until their end. Its number of verses is one hundred and nine verses. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ** (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) الر ** (1) Alif, Lām, Rā. Al-Qummi (said): الر ** is one of the letters of the Greatest Name (الاسم الأعظم) which is disjointed (المتقطع) in the Quran. If the Messenger (الرّسول) or the Imam (الإِمام) puts it together and supplicates with it, He is answered. I say: The like of this has preceded in the interpretation (تأويل) of الم ** at the beginning of Surah al-Baqarah. And in al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): And the meaning of الر ** is: I am Allah, the Compassionate (الرّؤف). تِلْكَ آياتُ الْكِتابِ الْحَكِيمِ ** These are the signs of the wise Book. Possessing wisdom (الحكمة) or whose verses are made firm (المحكم آياته).

(٢) أَكانَ لِلنَّاسِ عَجَباً أَنْ أَوْحَيْنا إِلى رَجُلٍ مِنْهُمْ ** (2) Was it a wonder to the people that We revealed to a man from among them This is a denial of their astonishment that He, the Mighty and the Majestic, sent a human being as a Messenger, as was previously mentioned in Surat al-An'am, or that He, glory be to Him, sent an orphan (يتيماً) who possessed neither status (جاه), nor wealth (مال), nor influence (بسطة). This stems from the extremity of their foolishness (حماقتهم), their limited focus on immediate matters (الأمور العاجلة), and their ignorance concerning the reality of revelation (الوحي) and prophethood (النّبوّة). أَنْ أَنْذِرِ النَّاسَ وَبَشِّرِ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا أَنَّ لَهُمْ قَدَمَ صِدْقٍ عِنْدَ رَبِّهِمْ ** that warn the people, and give good news to the faithful that they have a standing of truth with their Lord? Meaning: a precedence (سابقةً) and a favor (فضلاً). It was named qadam (foot/standing) because precedence (السّبق) is achieved by it, just as a blessing (النّعمة) is named yad (hand) because it is given by the hand. Its attribution (اضافاتها) to truth (الصّدق) is for its realization (لتحقِّقها) and to draw attention to the fact that they attain it through sincerity of speech (صدق القول) and intention (النّية). In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The meaning of قَدَمَ صِدْقٍ ** (a standing of truth) is the intercession (شفاعة) of Muhammad صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم. And in al-Kafi, al-'Ayyashi, and al-Qummi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): It is the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم. I say: This refers back to that (the previous interpretation). And in al-Kafi and al-'Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): It is through the guardianship (بولاية) of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). I say: This is because the guardianship (الولاية) is one of the conditions for intercession (الشفاعة), and the two are inseparable (متلازمان). قالَ الْكافِرُونَ إِنَّ هذا ** The faithless said, 'This meaning the Book and what the Messenger brought, لَسِحْرٌ مُبِينٌ (is indeed a manifest magic). And it was read (وقرئ) as لساحر (is indeed a sorcerer), based on the understanding that the reference (الإشارة) is to the Messenger. In this (statement) is an admission that they encountered from him extraordinary matters (أموراً خارقة للعادة) which rendered them incapable (معجزة إيّاهم) of opposition (المعارضة).

(3) إِنَّ رَبَّكُمُ اللهُ الَّذِي خَلَقَ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ فِي سِتَّةِ أَيَّامٍ ثُمَّ اسْتَوى عَلَى الْعَرْشِ (3) Indeed your Lord is Allah, who created the heavens and the earth in six days, and then settled on the Throne. Its interpretation has already preceded in Surat al-A'raf when the verse of subjugation (آية السّخرة) was mentioned. يُدَبِّرُ الْأَمْرَ (He directs the command) He decrees it, executes it, and arranges it in its stages according to the determinations of its outcomes. And al-Tadbir (التدبير) (directing/managing) is the consideration of the ends (ادبار) of affairs so that they may come with praiseworthy outcomes. And the command (الأمر) is the command of the entire creation. ما مِنْ شَفِيعٍ إِلَّا مِنْ بَعْدِ إِذْنِهِ (There is no intercessor except after His permission) This is an affirmation of His greatness and the might of His majesty, and a refutation against those who claimed that their deities intercede for them with Allah. ذلِكُمُ اللهُ (That is Allah) meaning, the One described with those attributes which necessitate divinity (الألوهيّة) and Lordship (الرّبوبيّة). رَبَّكُمُ (your Lord) and none other, since no one shares with Him in any part of that. فَاعْبُدُوهُ (so worship Him) alone, and do not associate anything with Him. أَفَلا تَذَكَّرُونَ (Will you not then take admonition?) meaning that the slightest remembrance (تذكّر) alerts one to the error in what you are upon, and to the fact that He is the One deserving of worship, not what you worship.

(4) إِلَيْهِ مَرْجِعُكُمْ جَمِيعاً ** (To Him will be your return, all of you) To Him is your return in the ultimate outcome, so prepare for meeting Him. وَعْدَ اللهِ حَقًّا (a promise of Allah, true). He promised a true promise. إِنَّهُ يَبْدَؤُا الْخَلْقَ ثُمَّ يُعِيدُهُ لِيَجْزِيَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ بِالْقِسْطِ (Indeed, He originates the creation, then He brings it back, that He may reward those who have faith and do righteous deeds with justice) by His justice, or by their justice in their affairs. وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لَهُمْ شَرابٌ مِنْ حَمِيمٍ وَعَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ بِما كانُوا يَكْفُرُونَ ** (But as for those who are faithless, there will be a drink of boiling water for them, and a painful punishment because of what they used to disbelieve.) It was said that the structure (النّظم) was changed for emphasis on their deserving of the punishment and to draw attention that the primary inherent purpose (بِالذات) of originating and repeating [creation] is the reward (الإثابة), while the punishment occurs incidentally (بِالْعَرْض). And that He, the Exalted, undertakes the rewarding (إثابة) of the believers with what is befitting to His kindness (لُطْف) and generosity (كَرَم), and for that reason, He did not specify it [the reward]. As for the punishment of the disbelievers, it is as if it is an ailment (داء) driven to them by their evil belief (سوء اعتقادهم) and the ill-omen (شؤم) of their deeds.

(5) هُوَ الَّذِي جَعَلَ الشَّمْسَ ضِياءً ** (5) He is the One who made the sun a radiance And it was read with two hamzahs (بهمزتين) wherever it occurs. وَالْقَمَرَ نُوراً وَقَدَّرَهُ مَنازِلَ and the moon a light, and determined for it stations. And He determined the moon as possessing stations (ذا منازل), or He determined its course (مسيره) as stations (منازل). And this is like His saying, glory be to Him: وَالْقَمَرَ قَدَّرْناهُ مَنازِلَ لِتَعْلَمُوا عَدَدَ السِّنِينَ وَالْحِسابَ ** and the moon We have determined its stations, so that you may know the number of years and the reckoning. [Meaning] the calculation of times (حساب الأوقات) concerning months (الأشهر), days (الأيّام), and nights (الليالي). ما خَلَقَ اللهُ ذلِكَ إِلَّا بِالْحَقِ ** Allah did not create that except with the truth. which is the perfect wisdom (الحكمة البالغة). يُفَصِّلُ الْآياتِ لِقَوْمٍ يَعْلَمُونَ He elaborates the signs for a people who have knowledge. And it was read with the yā' (بالياءِ), for they are the ones who benefit (المنتفعُون) from contemplating them.

(6) إِنَّ فِي اخْتِلافِ اللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهارِ وَما (١) خَلَقَ اللهُ فِي السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ لَآياتٍ لِقَوْمٍ يَتَّقُونَ (6) Indeed, in the alternation of the night and the day, and what Allah has created in the heavens and the earth, there are surely signs for a people who are wary of the consequences (العواقب). (7) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ لا يَرْجُونَ لِقاءَنا do not anticipate it, due to their denial of the Resurrection (البعث) and their preoccupation with perceptible things (بالمحسوسات) over what lies beyond them وَرَضُوا بِالْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا instead of the Hereafter (الآخِرة), due to their heedlessness of it وَاطْمَأَنُّوا بِها and they are tranquil toward it, with the tranquility of one who will not be disturbed/removed from it وَالَّذِينَ هُمْ عَنْ آياتِنا غافِلُونَ (٢) they are absent from contemplating them, heedless of looking into them.

(8) أُولئِكَ مَأْواهُمُ النَّارُ بِما كانُوا يَكْسِبُونَ ** (8) Those—their refuge is the Fire because of what they used to earn. Because of what they persisted in and trained themselves upon of acts of disobedience (المعاصي). (9) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ يَهْدِيهِمْ رَبُّهُمْ بِإِيمانِهِمْ ** (9) Indeed, those who have faith and do righteous deeds—their Lord guides them by their faith. Due to their faith, for steadfastness upon traversing the path that leads to Paradise (الجنّة). **تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهِمُ الْأَنْهارُ فِي جَنَّاتِ النَّعِيمِ ** ...streams run in gardens of bliss beneath them. Because adherence to the means of happiness is like reaching it.

(١٠) دَعْواهُمْ فِيها سُبْحانَكَ اللهُمَ ** (10) Their prayer therein will be: 'Immaculate are You, O Allah!' Their supplication therein (is): O Allah, indeed we declare Your transcendence (نُسَبِّحُكَ) with a glorification (تسبيحاً). Al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): that He (peace be upon him) was asked about al-tasbih (التسبيح). He said: "It is a Name (اسم) among the Names (أسماء) of Allah, the Exalted (تعالى), and the prayer (دعوى) of the people of Paradise (أهل الجنّة)." وَتَحِيَّتُهُمْ فِيها سَلامٌ وَآخِرُ دَعْواهُمْ ** and their greeting therein will be: 'Peace,' and the conclusion of their prayer will be: and the conclusion (خاتمة) of their supplication (دعائهم) أَنِ الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ رَبِّ الْعالَمِينَ. 'All praise belongs to Allah, the Lord of all the worlds.'

(١١) وَلَوْ يُعَجِّلُ اللهُ لِلنَّاسِ الشَّرَّ ** (11) If Allah were to hasten the evil for mankind) which they prayed for when distressed (ضجر) or insolent (بطر), like their saying: "May Allah remove me from among you," and like their saying: فَأَمْطِرْ عَلَيْنا حِجارَةً مِنَ السَّماءِ ** (then rain down upon us stones from the sky), or the evil which they deserved, اسْتِعْجالَهُمْ بِالْخَيْرِ ** (as they hasten the good), just as He hastens the good for them and responds to them regarding it when they hasten it. It was said: The phrase اسْتِعْجالَهُمْ بِالْخَيْرِ (as they hasten the good) was placed in the position of His hastening the good for them, indicating the swiftness of His response to them regarding the good, such that their hastening of it was [equivalent to] a hastening for them. لَقُضِيَ إِلَيْهِمْ أَجَلُهُمْ ** (their term would have been concluded for them) meaning they would have been killed and destroyed. And it was read (وقرئ) as لَقَضَى (لَقَضَى) based on the active voice (البناءِ للفاعل). Al-Qummi said: وَلَوْ يُعَجِّلُ اللهُ ** (If Allah were to hasten) for them الشَّرَّ ** (the evil) just as they hasten the good, لَقُضِيَ إِلَيْهِمْ أَجَلُهُمْ ** (their term would have been concluded for them), meaning their term would have been finished (فرغ من أجلهم). فَنَذَرُ الَّذِينَ لا يَرْجُونَ لِقاءَنا فِي طُغْيانِهِمْ يَعْمَهُونَ ** (So We leave those who do not expect to meet Us to wander perplexed in their transgression.) Meaning: We do not hasten the evil for them, nor do We conclude their term for them; rather, We grant them respite (نمهّلهم امهالاً).

(١٢) وَإِذا مَسَّ الْإِنْسانَ الضُّرُّ دَعانا ** (12) When distress befalls man, he supplicates Us to repel it, being sincere therein لِجَنْبِهِ ** lying on his side, meaning, reclining (مضطجعاً) (١) أَوْ قاعِداً أَوْ قائِماً ** or sitting, or standing. meaning that he does not cease supplicating in all his states, he does not slacken until the distress is removed from him. فَلَمَّا كَشَفْنا عَنْهُ ضُرَّهُ مَرَّ ** But when We remove his distress from him, he passes on upon his first way before the distress afflicted him, or he passes away from the station of supplication and humility, not returning to it كَأَنْ لَمْ يَدْعُنا ** as if he had not supplicated Us as if he لَمْ يَدْعُنا إِلى ضُرٍّ مَسَّهُ ** (had not supplicated Us regarding the distress that afflicted him) [after] the removal of the distress. كَذلِكَ ** Thus like that adornment زُيِّنَ لِلْمُسْرِفِينَ ما كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ ** is what they have been doing made fair-seeming to the extravagant. which is the immersion in desires (الشّهوات) and the turning away from acts of worship (العبادات) during prosperity (الرّخاءِ).

(١٣) وَلَقَدْ أَهْلَكْنَا الْقُرُونَ مِنْ قَبْلِكُمْ لَمَّا ظَلَمُوا ** (13) Certainly We destroyed the generations before you when they did wrong, by denial (بالتكذيب). وَجاءَتْهُمْ رُسُلُهُمْ بِالْبَيِّناتِ ** and their apostles brought them manifest proofs, with the arguments (بالحجج) indicating their truthfulness (الدالة على صدقهم). وَما كانُوا لِيُؤْمِنُوا ** and they would not have believed, due to the corruption of their disposition (لفساد استعدادهم) and Allah's abandonment (وخذلان الله) of them, because of His knowledge of their persistence (بإصرارهم) in disbelief (على الكفر), and that there was no benefit (لا فائدة) in granting them respite (في إمهالهم) after the proof (الحجّة) had been established against them (لزمهم) by the sending of the apostles (بإرسال الرسل). كَذلِكَ نَجْزِي الْقَوْمَ الْمُجْرِمِينَ ** Thus do We requite the guilty people. every guilty person (كل مجرم).

(١٤) ثُمَّ جَعَلْناكُمْ خَلائِفَ فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (14) Then We made you successors in the land We made you successors in the earth مِنْ بَعْدِهِمْ ** after them, after the generations whom We destroyed لِنَنْظُرَ كَيْفَ تَعْمَلُونَ ** so that We may observe how you will fare. whether good or evil. (١٥) وَإِذا تُتْلى عَلَيْهِمْ آياتُنا بَيِّناتٍ قالَ الَّذِينَ لا يَرْجُونَ لِقاءَنَا ائْتِ بِقُرْآنٍ غَيْرِ هذا ** (15) When Our manifest signs are recited to them, those who do not expect to meet Us say, 'Bring a Qur'an other than this, another Quran in which there is nothing that angers us, such as the condemnation of idol worship and the threat of punishment to their worshippers. أَوْ بَدِّلْهُ ** or change it.' by making a verse of mercy in place of a verse of punishment, and dropping the mention of the deities (الآلهة) and the condemnation of their worship. قُلْ ما يَكُونُ (٢) لِي (٣) ما يصحّ لي Say, 'It is not for me أَنْ أُبَدِّلَهُ مِنْ تِلْقاءِ نَفْسِي ** to change it on my own. from my own accord, without my Lord commanding me to do so. إِنْ أَتَّبِعُ إِلَّا ما يُوحى إِلَيَ I follow only what is revealed to me. Changing or abrogating (نسخ) is not up to me. إِنِّي أَخافُ إِنْ عَصَيْتُ رَبِّي ** Indeed, should I disobey my Lord, regarding changing or abrogating (نسخ) from my own accord, عَذابَ يَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍ.** I fear the punishment of a tremendous day.'

footnote (١) Meaning, the sick person (*العليل*) who is unable to sit, **أَوْ قاعِداً ** (or sitting) who is unable to stand, **أَوْ قائِماً ** (or standing) who is healthy (*الصّحيح*).

footnote (٢) In *al-Kafi*, *al-Qummi*, and *al-Ayyashi*, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): They said, "And change Ali (peace be upon him) from it (the Quran)." May Allah have mercy on him.

footnote (٣) And whoever uses this verse as proof that the abrogation (*نسخ*) of the Quran by the *Sunnah* is impermissible has gone far afield, because if the Quran is abrogated by the *Sunnah* and what the Prophet says...

(١٦) قُلْ لَوْ شاءَ اللهُ ما تَلَوْتُهُ عَلَيْكُمْ وَلا أَدْراكُمْ بِهِ ** (16) Say, 'Had Allah willed, I would not have recited it to you, nor would He have made it known to you.' And Allah would not have informed you of it through my tongue. And it was recited (ولأدريكم) with the lām of emphasis (لام التأكيد), meaning: And He would have informed you of it through the tongue of someone else. This means that its recitation is only by the Will of Allah and His bringing forth (احداثه) an astonishing matter (أمراً عجيباً) that breaks the norm (خارقاً للعادة), which is that an unlettered man (رجل أمّي) emerges, who has not learned for an hour of his life, nor grown up (نشأ) (1) in a land where scholars reside, yet he recites to you a Book whose eloquence overwhelms every eloquent speech, imbued (مسحوناً) with the knowledge of what was and what will be. فَقَدْ لَبِثْتُ فِيكُمْ عُمَراً مِنْ قَبْلِهِ ** (16) I have certainly lived among you for a lifetime before it. I have certainly resided among you, growing up and as a mature man (كهلاً), for a period of forty years, yet you did not know me to be engaged in anything of that nature, so that you might accuse me of inventing it. أَفَلا تَعْقِلُونَ ** (16) Will you not then exercise your reason? Do you not employ your intellects through contemplation (التدبّر) and reflection (التفكّر) so that you may know that it is only from Allah?

(١٧) فَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّنِ افْتَرى عَلَى اللهِ كَذِباً أَوْ كَذَّبَ بِآياتِهِ إِنَّهُ لا يُفْلِحُ الْمُجْرِمُونَ. (17) So who is a greater wrongdoer than him who fabricates a lie against Allah, or denies His signs? Indeed, the guilty will not be felicitous. (١٨) وَيَعْبُدُونَ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ ما لا يَضُرُّهُمْ وَلا يَنْفَعُهُمْ وَيَقُولُونَ هؤُلاءِ شُفَعاؤُنا عِنْدَ اللهِ تشفع لنا فيما يهمنا من أمور الدنيا والآخِرة قُلْ أَتُنَبِّئُونَ اللهَ بِما لا يَعْلَمُ فِي السَّماواتِ وَلا فِي الْأَرْضِ أتخبرونه بما ليس بمعلوم للعالم بجميع المعلومات يعني بما ليس بموجود سُبْحانَهُ وَتَعالى عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ وقرئ بالتاءِ (18) They worship besides Allah what neither harms them nor benefits them, and they say, 'These are our intercessors with Allah.' They intercede for us concerning what concerns us regarding the affairs of this world and the Hereafter. قُلْ أَتُنَبِّئُونَ اللهَ بِما لا يَعْلَمُ فِي السَّماواتِ وَلا فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (Say, 'Do you inform Allah about what He does not know in the heavens or on the earth?') Do you inform Him about what is not known to the Knower of all information (المعلومات), meaning, about what is not existent (بما ليس بموجود)? سُبْحانَهُ وَتَعالى عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ ** (Immaculate is He, and exalted above what they associate [with Him]!) And it was recited with the tā’ (وقرئ بالتاءِ). القمّيّ said: Quraysh used to worship idols and say: "We only worship them so that they may bring us nearer to Allah in proximity (زلفى), for we are unable to worship Allah." So Allah refuted them and said: Say to them, O Muhammad: أَتُنَبِّئُونَ اللهَ بِما لا يَعْلَمُ ** (Do you inform Allah about what He does not know?), meaning, He does not know [it]. Thus, a letter (حرفاً) was placed in the stead of a letter (حرف), meaning: He does not have a partner who is worshipped.

(١٩) وَما كانَ النَّاسُ إِلَّا أُمَّةً واحِدَةً ** (19) And mankind were none but a single nation meaning, before the sending of Noah (نوح), they were upon the primordial nature (الفطرة), neither guided nor astray, as its explanation preceded in Surat al-Baqarah during the exegesis of this word. Then they differed (فَاخْتَلَفُوا) by following caprice (الهوى) and by the sending of the Messengers (الرسل). So, one group followed them, and another group turned away. وَلَوْ لا كَلِمَةٌ سَبَقَتْ مِنْ رَبِّكَ ** And were it not for a word that went before from your Lord (which is) the deferment of judgment between them until the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة), judgment would have been passed between them immediately فِيما فِيهِ يَخْتَلِفُونَ ** concerning that about which they differ. And the truthful would have been distinguished from the false (المبطِل). However, wisdom necessitated that this abode (هذه الدار) be for obligation (التكليف) and trial (الاختبار), and that the other (abode) be for reward (الثّواب) and punishment (العقاب).

footnote صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم for He only speaks by the revelation (*الوحي*) from Allah. He did not abrogate the Quran nor change it from His own accord. Rather, His changing it is from Allah, the Exalted (*تعالى*), but it would not be the Quran. This is supported by His saying: **وَمَا يَنطِقُ عَنِ الْهَوَى (3) إِنْ هُوَ إِلَّا وَحْيٌ يُوحَى (4)** (And He does not speak out of [his own] desire. (3) It is merely a revelation that is revealed [to him], (4)).

footnote (١) *نشأ* (to grow up) is like *منع* (to prevent/forbid) [in conjugation], meaning *شبّ* (to grow up/mature). And *النّاشئ* (the growing one) is the youth (*الغلام*) who has passed the limit of childhood (*حدّ الصّغر*). (*ق*).

(٢٠) **وَيَقُولُونَ لَوْ لا أُنْزِلَ عَلَيْهِ آيَةٌ مِنْ رَبِّهِ ** (20) And they say, 'Why has not a sign been sent down to him from his Lord?'

i.e., from the signs which they proposed. فَقُلْ إِنَّمَا الْغَيْبُ لِلَّهِ (then say: 'The Unseen belongs only to Allah'); He is the One specialized in its knowledge. وَلِكُلِّ أَمْرٍ أَجَلٌ فَانْتَظِرُوا (and for every matter there is an appointed time. So wait) for the descent of what you proposed. إِنِّي مَعَكُمْ مِنَ الْمُنْتَظِرِينَ (I am with you among the waiters) for what Allah will do to you.

(٢١) وَإِذا أَذَقْنَا النَّاسَ رَحْمَةً صحة وسعة مِنْ بَعْدِ ضَرَّاءَ مَسَّتْهُمْ كمرض وقحط إِذا لَهُمْ مَكْرٌ فاجأوا وقوع المكر منهم فِي آياتِنا بالطعن والاحتيال في دفعها (21) When We let the people taste mercy after a distress that has befallen them, behold, they scheme against Our signs.

(Meaning) health (صحة) and abundance (سعة). (After a distress that has befallen them) such as illness (كمرض) and drought (وقحط). (Behold, they scheme) meaning the occurrence of scheming (المكر) from them suddenly appears (فاجأوا وقوع المكر منهم). (Against Our signs) by means of slander (بالطعن) and deception (والاحتيال) in repelling them (في دفعها).

It was said (قيل) that the people of Mecca suffered drought (قحط) for seven years until they were almost destroyed. Then, when Allah showed them mercy (رحمهم الله) with rain (بالمطر), they began to slander (يقدحون) the signs of Allah and plot against His Messenger (ويكيدون رسوله).

قُلِ اللهُ أَسْرَعُ مَكْراً منكم قد دبّر عقابكم قبل أن تدبّروا كيدكم والمكر إخفاء الكيد وهو من الله تعالى الاستدراج والجزاء على المكر Say: 'Allah is swifter in scheming.'

(He is swifter in scheming) than you (منكم). He has planned your punishment (عقابكم) before you planned your plot (كيدكم). And al-Makr (المكر) is the concealment of the plot (إخفاء الكيد). And from Allah, the Exalted (تعالى), it is the gradual leading astray (الاستدراج) and the recompense for the scheming (والجزاء على المكر).

إِنَّ رُسُلَنا يَكْتُبُونَ ما تَمْكُرُونَ اعلام بأنّ ما يظنونه خافياً غيرَ خاف على الله وتحقيق للانتقام. Indeed Our messengers write down what you scheme.

This is a notification (اعلام) that what they deem hidden (خافياً) is not hidden from Allah (غيرَ خاف على الله), and it is a confirmation of the retribution (وتحقيق للانتقام).

(٢٢) هُوَ الَّذِي يُسَيِّرُكُمْ ** He carries you upon travel and enables you thereof by preparing its means فِي الْبَرِّ وَالْبَحْرِ حَتَّى إِذا كُنْتُمْ فِي الْفُلْكِ ** in the ships. وَجَرَيْنَ بِهِمْ and they (the ships) sailed with them (those who are in them). He shifted from addressing (the second person plural) to the third person plural (الْغَيْبَة) for emphasis (لِلْمُبَالَغَة), as if He is mentioning it to others so that they may wonder at their condition, بِرِيحٍ طَيِّبَةٍ gentle in its blowing, وَفَرِحُوا بِها and they rejoiced in it, in that wind. جاءَتْها it came to them (the ships) رِيحٌ عاصِفٌ ** severe in its blowing وَجاءَهُمُ الْمَوْجُ مِنْ كُلِّ مَكانٍ ** from the places of the waves وَظَنُّوا أَنَّهُمْ أُحِيطَ بِهِمْ ** meaning, they were destroyed/perished; that is, the paths of salvation were blocked for them, like one whom the enemy has surrounded, and this is a metaphor for destruction (الْهَلَاك). دَعَوُا اللهَ مُخْلِصِينَ لَهُ الدِّينَ ** because at that time they do not invoke anyone other than Him along with Him, **لَئِنْ أَنْجَيْتَنا مِنْ هذِهِ لَنَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الشَّاكِرِينَ ** intending the saying (i.e., the meaning is that they said this).

هُوَ الَّذِي يُسَيِّرُكُمْ فِي الْبَرِّ وَالْبَحْرِ حَتَّى إِذا كُنْتُمْ فِي الْفُلْكِ وَجَرَيْنَ بِهِمْ بِرِيحٍ طَيِّبَةٍ وَفَرِحُوا بِها جاءَتْها رِيحٌ عاصِفٌ وَجاءَهُمُ الْمَوْجُ مِنْ كُلِّ مَكانٍ وَظَنُّوا أَنَّهُمْ أُحِيطَ بِهِمْ دَعَوُا اللهَ مُخْلِصِينَ لَهُ الدِّينَ لَئِنْ أَنْجَيْتَنا مِنْ هذِهِ لَنَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الشَّاكِرِينَ (٢٢) (It is He who conveys you on land and sea. When you are in the ships, and they sail with them with a good wind, and they rejoice in it, there comes upon them a violent wind, and the waves come at them from every side, and they think that they are encompassed. They invoke Allah, dedicating their faith to Him: 'If You deliver us from this, we will surely be among the grateful.')

فَلَمَّا أَنْجاهُمْ (٢٣) (When He delivered them) in response to their supplication, إِذا هُمْ يَبْغُونَ فِي الْأَرْضِ (behold, they commit aggression in the land). They suddenly engaged in corruption therein and hastened back to what they were upon, بِغَيْرِ الْحَقِ ** (unjustly), acting falsely therein. And this is a precaution against the destruction by the Muslims of the lands of the disbelievers (ديار الكفرة), for that is corruption (إفساد) with right (بحقّ). يا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ إِنَّما بَغْيُكُمْ عَلى أَنْفُسِكُمْ ** (O mankind! Your aggression is only against yourselves), for its evil consequence (وباله) is upon you, or it is upon your likes and the children of your kind (أبناء جنسكم). مَتاعَ الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا ** (the enjoyment of the life of the world). The benefit (منفعة) of the worldly life does not remain, but its punishment remains. And it is the predicate (خبر) of بَغْيُكُمْ ** or a suppressed predicate. And it was read with the nasb (النّصب), meaning: they enjoy the enjoyment of the worldly life.

Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Three things revert upon their perpetrator: breaking a covenant (النكث), aggression (البغي), and plotting (المكر). Then He recited this verse. ثُمَّ إِلَيْنا مَرْجِعُكُمْ فَنُنَبِّئُكُمْ بِما كُنْتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ. (Then to Us will be your return, whereat We will inform you concerning what you used to do.)

(24) إِنَّما مَثَلُ الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا (24) The parable of the life of this world is only Its wondrous state (حالها العجيبة) in the swiftness of its passing (سرعة تفضّيها), and the departure of its bliss (ذهاب نعيمها) after its approach (إقبالها), and the delusion of people by it (واغترار النّاس بها) كَماءٍ أَنْزَلْناهُ مِنَ السَّماءِ فَاخْتَلَطَ بِهِ نَباتُ الْأَرْضِ مِمَّا يَأْكُلُ النَّاسُ وَالْأَنْعامُ (like) the rain that We send down from the sky, whereupon the vegetation of the earth, which humans and cattle eat, mingles with it. such as crops (الزروع), vegetables (البقول), and grass (الحشيش). حَتَّى إِذا أَخَذَتِ الْأَرْضُ زُخْرُفَها [meaning] its beauty (زينتها) وَازَّيَّنَتْ and becomes adorned (وتزيّنت) with various kinds (أصناف) of plants, and their different forms (أشكالها) and colors (ألوانها), like a bride who takes various colors of garments (الثياب) and adornments (الزّين) and beautifies herself with them. وَظَنَّ أَهْلُها أَنَّهُمْ قادِرُونَ عَلَيْها and its inhabitants think that they have power over it, [meaning] they are capable (متمكّنون) of harvesting it (حصدها) and gathering its yield (رفع غلّتها) أَتاها أَمْرُنا Our command comes to it [meaning] a blight (عاهة) and a pestilence (آفة) strikes it, after their security (أَمْنهم) and certainty (ايقانهم) that it had been saved لَيْلاً أَوْ نَهاراً فَجَعَلْناها by night or day, and We turn it into so We make its crop (زرعها) حَصِيداً a mown field, resembling what is harvested (يحصد) from the crop (الزرع) at its root (أصله) كَأَنْ لَمْ تَغْنَ بِالْأَمْسِ as if it had not flourished yesterday. as if its crop (زرعها) had not existed (لم يوجد) in the time preceding it (فيما قبله). And al-Ams (الأمس) is a metaphor (مثل) for the near time (الوقت القريب). And that which is being likened (الممثّل به) in the Ayah is the content of the narrative (مضمون الحكاية), which is the sudden disappearance of the plant's greenery (زوال خضرة النبات فجأة) and its becoming debris (حطاماً) (1) after it was fresh (غضّاً), intertwined (التفّ), and adorned the earth, until its inhabitants coveted it (طمع فيه أهله) and thought that it had been saved from pests (الآفات); not the water (لا الماءِ), even though the particle of similitude (حرف التشبيه) precedes it, because it is a compound similitude (التشبيه المركب). كَذلِكَ نُفَصِّلُ الْآياتِ لِقَوْمٍ يَتَفَكَّرُونَ Thus do We elaborate the signs for a people who reflect. for they are the ones who benefit from it (المنتفعون به).

(٢٥) وَاللهُ يَدْعُوا إِلى دارِ السَّلامِ ** (25) Allah invites to the abode of peace meaning the abode of Allah. In al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding this verse, He said that السَّلامِ ** (peace) is Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ), and His abode which He created for His servants and His friends (أوليائه) is Paradise (الجنّة). وَيَهْدِي مَنْ يَشاءُ ** and guides whomever He wishes by granting divine success (التوفيق). إِلى صِراطٍ مُسْتَقِيمٍ to a straight path.** which is its path.

footnote (١) الحطام ما يحطم عن عيدان الزّرع إذا يبس من حطم الشّيء حطماً من باب تعب إذا انكسر وحطمه حطماً من باب ضرب فانحطم م. (1) الحطام (al-Hutām) is that which is broken off (يُحطَم) from the stalks of the crop when it dries up. It derives from حَطَمَ (ḥaṭama) (a verbal noun حَطْماً from the pattern تَعِبَ), meaning when the thing broke, and حَطَمَهُ (ḥaṭamahu) (a verbal noun حَطْماً from the pattern ضَرَبَ), meaning he broke it, so it became broken (فَانْحَطَمَ).

(٢٦) لِلَّذِينَ أَحْسَنُوا الْحُسْنى وَزِيادَةٌ ** (26) For those who are virtuous is the best reward and an increase. The excellent reward (المثوبة الحسنى), and an increase (وَزِيادَةٌ), and what exceeds the reward as a favor (تفضلاً). Al-Qummi said: It is looking upon the mercy of Allah. And narrated from Al-Baqir (peace be upon him): As for the best reward (الْحُسْنى), it is Paradise, and as for the increase (الزيادة), it is the worldly life (الدنيا)—what Allah granted them in the worldly life, He will not hold them accountable for it in the Hereafter, and He will combine for them the reward (ثواب) of the worldly life and the Hereafter. And in al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): The increase (الزيادة) is a chamber (غرفة) made of a single pearl (لؤلؤة واحدة) having four doors.

(٢٧) وَلا يَرْهَقُ وُجُوهَهُمْ Nor will it envelop them قَتَرٌ dust (غبرة) containing blackness (سواد) وَلا ذِلَّةٌ the trace of humiliation (أثر هوان). أُولئِكَ أَصْحابُ الْجَنَّةِ هُمْ فِيها خالِدُونَ (They are the inhabitants of paradise, and they will remain in it [forever]) meaning: everlasting (دائمون); there is no vanishing therein, nor is there an end (انقراض) to its bliss. وَالَّذِينَ كَسَبُوا السَّيِّئاتِ جَزاءُ سَيِّئَةٍ بِمِثْلِها ** (But those who commit misdeeds, the requital of a misdeed is its like,) meaning: a misdeed is requited with a misdeed similar to it; nothing is added to it. And in this is an indication (دلالة) that what is meant by the increase (الزيادة) [in the previous verse] is grace (الفضل). وَتَرْهَقُهُمْ ذِلَّةٌ ما لَهُمْ مِنَ اللهِ مِنْ عاصِمٍ ** (and abasement will cover them. They will have no defender against Allah.) meaning: no one will protect them (يعصمهم) from the wrath (سخط) of Allah and His punishment, or [it means] they will have no one from Allah Who protects them (يعصمهم), as is the case for the believers. كَأَنَّما أُغْشِيَتْ وُجُوهُهُمْ قِطَعاً مِنَ اللَّيْلِ مُظْلِماً ** (As if their faces were covered with patches of the dark night.) due to the intensity of their blackness (سوادها) and darkness (ظلمتها). And قِطَعاً was read with the sukūn on the letter ط (بسكون الطاءِ). أُولئِكَ أَصْحابُ النَّارِ هُمْ فِيها خالِدُونَ.** (They are the inhabitants of the Fire, and they will remain in it [forever].)

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): These are the people of innovations (البدع), doubts (الشبهات), and desires (الشهوات). Allah will blacken their faces, and then they will meet Him. He (peace be upon him) said: And He will clothe them with humiliation (الذلة) and abasement (الصّغار).

And in al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Do you not see that when the house is night, it becomes intensely black (أشدّ سواداً)? Likewise, they will increase in blackness (سواداً).

(٢٨) وَيَوْمَ نَحْشُرُهُمْ جَمِيعاً (And the day We shall muster them all) meaning the two groups (or parties). ثُمَّ نَقُولُ لِلَّذِينَ أَشْرَكُوا مَكانَكُمْ (then We shall say to those who associated partners [with Allah]: 'Keep to your places,') Adhere to your places, do not depart until you see what is done to you. أَنْتُمْ وَشُرَكاؤُكُمْ فَزَيَّلْنا بَيْنَهُمْ (you and your partners.') Then We shall separate them.) So We separated them and cut off the connection that was between them. And al-Qummi (narrates): Allah sends forth a fire that separates (1) between the disbelievers and the believers. وَقالَ شُرَكاؤُهُمْ ما كُنْتُمْ إِيَّانا تَعْبُدُونَ (And their partners will say, 'You did not worship us.') Because, in reality, they only worshipped their desires (أهوائهم) which carried them to polytheism (الإشراك), not that which they associated as partners with Him, or (they worshipped) the devils (الشياطين) when they commanded them to take rivals (أنداداً) to Allah, and they obeyed them.

footnote (١) I separated it (*زيّلته*) and it became separated (*فتزّيل*), meaning: I divided it (*فرّقته*) and it became divided (*فتفرّق*). (*ص.*)

(٢٩) فَكَفى بِاللهِ شَهِيداً بَيْنَنا وَبَيْنَكُمْ ** (29) Allah suffices as a witness between us and you. For He is the Knower of the essence of the matter. إِنْ كُنَّا ** meaning: that we were عَنْ عِبادَتِكُمْ لَغافِلِينَ. (unaware of your worship.) (٣٠) هُنالِكَ ** in that station (المقام) تَبْلُوا كُلُّ نَفْسٍ ما أَسْلَفَتْ ** it tests what it put forth of deeds, so it witnesses its benefit and harm. And it was recited (تَتْلُو), meaning it reads (تَقْرَأ) from recitation (التلاوة) or it follows (تَتْبَع) from following (التّلو). وَرُدُّوا إِلَى اللهِ مَوْلاهُمُ الْحَقِّ ** their Lord Whose Lordship (ربوبيّته) is true, the One Who manages their affair in reality, not what they took as a master (مولى). وَضَلَّ عَنْهُمْ ** and what they lost/vanished from them **ما كانُوا يَفْتَرُونَ ** they claimed that they were partners of Allah and that they would intercede for them. (30) There every soul shall test what it has sent ahead, and they shall be returned to Allah, their true Master, and what they used to fabricate will forsake them.

(31) قُلْ مَنْ يَرْزُقُكُمْ مِنَ السَّماءِ وَالْأَرْضِ (31) Say, ‘Who provides for you from the heaven and the earth?’ all of you, through heavenly and earthly means. أَمَّنْ يَمْلِكُ السَّمْعَ وَالْأَبْصارَ (Or who controls the hearing and the sights?) Who is able to create them (خلقهما), perfect them (تسويتهما), and preserve them (حفظهما) from afflictions (الآفات), despite their abundance and their rapid susceptibility to the slightest thing? وَمَنْ يُخْرِجُ الْحَيَّ مِنَ الْمَيِّتِ وَيُخْرِجُ الْمَيِّتَ مِنَ الْحَيِ (and who brings forth the living from the dead, and brings forth the dead from the living?) Who gives life (يحيي) and causes death (يميت)? وَمَنْ يُدَبِّرُ الْأَمْرَ (and who directs the command?) And who undertakes the management (تدبير) of the affair (أمر) of the universe (العالم)? فَسَيَقُولُونَ اللهُ (They will say, ‘Allah.’) since they are unable to show arrogance (المكابرة) or obstinacy (العناد) regarding that, due to its extreme clarity. Then say: فَقُلْ أَفَلا تَتَّقُونَ (Then will you not be wary) of His punishment (عقابه) by worshipping other than Him?

(٣٢) فَذلِكُمُ اللهُ رَبُّكُمُ الْحَقُ ** (32) That is Allah, your true Lord. i.e., the One Who manages these affairs and is deserving of worship is your Lord, Whose Lordship is established, because He is the One Who originated you, gave you life, provided for you, and managed your affairs. فَما ذا بَعْدَ الْحَقِّ إِلَّا الضَّلالُ ** (What is there after the truth except error?) meaning there is no intermediary between the two, so whoever steps beyond the truth falls into error. **فَأَنَّى تُصْرَفُونَ ** (So where are you being turned away?) from the truth.

(٣٣) كَذلِكَ حَقَّتْ كَلِمَةُ رَبِّكَ ** (Thus the word of your Lord has become due) and His judgment (حُكْمِه) عَلَى الَّذِينَ فَسَقُوا ** (upon those who committed transgression) —they rebelled in their disbelief and departed from soundness (الرّشد)—أَنَّهُمْ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ (that they will not believe) is a substitute (بدل) for الكلمة (the word); meaning, the negation of faith became due upon them. Or, what is meant by الكلمة is the promise of punishment (العدة بالعذاب), and this is a justification (تعليل) for it. And it was read as كلمات (plural).

كَذلِكَ حَقَّتْ كَلِمَةُ رَبِّكَ عَلَى الَّذِينَ فَسَقُوا أَنَّهُمْ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ (Thus the word of your Lord became due against those who commit transgression, that they will not believe.)

(٣٤) **قُلْ هَلْ مِنْ شُرَكائِكُمْ مَنْ يَبْدَؤُا الْخَلْقَ ثُمَّ يُعِيدُهُ قُلِ اللهُ يَبْدَؤُا الْخَلْقَ ثُمَّ يُعِيدُهُ فَأَنَّى تُؤْفَكُونَ ** (34) Say, 'Is there anyone among your partners who originates the creation, and then brings it back?' Say, 'Allah originates the creation, and then brings it back. So where are you being led astray?'

He made the return (الإعادة) like the origination (الإبداء) in the necessity (الإلزام) of it, due to the manifestation of its proof (برهانها), even if they did not assent to it. And for that reason, He commanded the Messenger (الرّسول) to answer on their behalf in the response.

(٣٥) قُلْ هَلْ مِنْ شُرَكائِكُمْ مَنْ يَهْدِي إِلَى الْحَقِ ** (35) Say, 'Is there anyone among your partners who guides to the truth?' by establishing proofs (الحجج), sending the messengers (الرّسل), and granting success (التوفيق) for contemplation (النظر) and reflection (التدبر). قُلِ اللهُ يَهْدِي لِلْحَقِّ أَفَمَنْ يَهْدِي إِلَى الْحَقِّ أَحَقُّ أَنْ يُتَّبَعَ أَمَّنْ لا يَهِدِّي إِلَّا أَنْ يُهْدى ** Say, 'Allah guides to the truth.' Is He who guides to the truth worthier to be followed, or he who does not guide unless he is guided? [The meaning of] أَمَّنْ لا يَهِدِّي ** is: He is not guided (لا يهتدي). And it was recited with the fatḥah (فتح) on the hā' (الهاءِ) and the doubling (تشديد) of the dāl (الدّالّ), and with the kasrah (كسر) and the doubling. [The meaning of] إِلَّا أَنْ يُهْدى ** is: Someone else guides him (يهديه غيره).

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Baqir عليه السلام: As for مَنْ يَهْدِي إِلَى الْحَقِ ** (He who guides to the truth), it is Muḥammad and the Family of Muḥammad عليهم السلام after him. And as for أَمَّنْ لا يَهِدِّي ** (or he who does not guide), it is he who opposed his Household (أهل بيته) from Quraysh and others after him.

فَما لَكُمْ كَيْفَ تَحْكُمُونَ ** What is the matter with you? How do you judge? with falsehood (بالباطل).

(٣٦) وَما يَتَّبِعُ أَكْثَرُهُمْ فيما يعتقدون إِلَّا ظَنًّا مستنداً إلى خيالات فاسدة إِنَّ الظَّنَّ لا يُغْنِي مِنَ الْحَقِ من الإعتقاد الحقّ شَيْئاً إِنَّ اللهَ عَلِيمٌ بِما يَفْعَلُونَ ** (36) And most of them follow nothing but conjecture. Indeed, conjecture does not avail against the truth in any way. Indeed, Allah is knowing of what they do.**

وَما يَتَّبِعُ أَكْثَرُهُمْ ** (And most of them follow) in what they believe إِلَّا ظَنًّا ** (nothing but conjecture), based upon corrupt imaginations (خيالات فاسدة). إِنَّ الظَّنَّ لا يُغْنِي مِنَ الْحَقِ ** (Indeed, conjecture does not avail against the truth)—meaning, against the true belief (الإعتقاد الحقّ)—شَيْئاً إِنَّ اللهَ عَلِيمٌ بِما يَفْعَلُونَ ** (in any way. Indeed, Allah is knowing of what they do). (This is) a threat (وعيد) regarding their following conjecture (الظنّ) and their turning away (اعراضهم) from the clear proof (البرهان).

(٣٧) وَما كانَ ** (And it was not) And it was not sound (وما صح) and it was not proper (وما استقامَ) هذَا الْقُرْآنُ أَنْ يُفْتَرى مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ ** (for this Quran to be fabricated by anyone besides Allah) that it should be a fabrication (افتراء) from the creation (مِنَ الخلق) وَلكِنْ تَصْدِيقَ الَّذِي بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ ** (but a confirmation of what was before it) of the revealed Books (الكتب المنزلة), because it renders them incapable (يعجز دونها), and it is a standard (عيار) upon them, bearing witness (شاهد) to their validity (لصحّتها) وَتَفْصِيلَ الْكِتابِ ** (and an elaboration of the Book) and a clarification (وتبيين) of what He legislated (ما شرع) and obligated (وفرض) of the rulings (الأحكام) from His saying كِتابَ اللهِ عَلَيْكُمْ لا رَيْبَ فِيهِ مِنْ رَبِّ الْعالَمِينَ. (A Book from Allah upon you, there is no doubt in it, from the Lord of all the worlds.)

(٣٨) أَمْ يَقُولُونَ ** Rather, do they say? افْتَراهُ ** He forged it (اختلقه). قُلْ ** If I have forged it, as you claimed, then bring forth a surah (سُورَةٍ) like it in eloquence (البلاغة) and the excellence of composition (حسن النظم), by way of fabrication (على وجه الافتراء), for you are like me in Arabic language (العربيّة) and eloquence (الفصاحة). وَادْعُوا مَنِ اسْتَطَعْتُمْ ** whom you can call upon for assistance in bringing forth something like it, مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ (besides Allah)—meaning, other than Allah (سوى الله)—for He (فانّه) alone is capable of that, and no one else (لا غير). إِنْ كُنْتُمْ صادِقِينَ ** that it is a fabrication (انّه افتراء).

أَمْ يَقُولُونَ افْتَراهُ قُلْ فَأْتُوا بِسُورَةٍ مِثْلِهِ وَادْعُوا مَنِ اسْتَطَعْتُمْ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ صادِقِينَ (٣٨) (Or do they say, 'He has fabricated it'? Say, 'Then bring a surah like it, and call upon whomever you can, besides Allah, should you be truthful.') (38)

(٣٩) **بَلْ كَذَّبُوا بِما لَمْ يُحِيطُوا بِعِلْمِهِ وَلَمَّا يَأْتِهِمْ تَأْوِيلُهُ ** (39) Rather, they denied what they did not comprehend by knowledge, and whose interpretation has not yet come to them.

Rather, they denied the Quran before they knew the essence (كُنْه) of its matter and before they understood its interpretation (تَأْوِيل) and its meanings, due to their aversion to what contradicted what they were accustomed to from the religion of their fathers. Even though the interpretation (تَأْوِيل) of what is in it concerning the reports of the unseen (الغيوب)—that is, its consequence (عَاقِبَة)—has not yet come to them, when it will become clear to them whether it is a lie or the truth. This means that it is a miraculous Book (كِتَاب مُعْجِز) from two aspects: the miraculous nature (إِعْجَاز) of its composition (نَظْم) and what is in it concerning the reports of the unseen matters (الإِخْبَار بِالْغَائِبَات). So they hastened to denial (التَّكْذِيب) before they considered its attainment of the limit of miraculousness (حَدّ الإِعْجَاز) and before they tested its reports concerning the unseen matters (الْمُغَيَّبَات).

Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about the momentous matters (الأُمُور الْعِظَام), such as the Return (الرَّجْعَة) and others. He said: "Indeed, this about which you ask me has not yet come," or He said: "Allah said: **بَلْ كَذَّبُوا بِما لَمْ يُحِيطُوا بِعِلْمِهِ وَلَمَّا يَأْتِهِمْ تَأْوِيلُهُ ** (Rather, they denied what they did not comprehend by knowledge, and whose interpretation has not yet come to them)." And a similar narration is reported from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him).

And al-Qummi said: It was revealed concerning the Return (الرَّجْعَة). They denied it, meaning that it will not occur.

In al-Kafi, al-Majma', and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Allah specified this verse with two verses from His Book: that they should not say what they do not know, and that they should not reject what they do not know. Then He recited: أَلَمْ يُؤْخَذْ عَلَيْهِمْ مِيثاقُ الْكِتابِ أَنْ لا يَقُولُوا عَلَى اللهِ إِلَّا الْحَقَ (Was not the covenant of the Book taken from them that they shall not attribute to Allah anything but the truth?) and His saying: بَلْ كَذَّبُوا بِما لَمْ يُحِيطُوا بِعِلْمِهِ وَلَمَّا يَأْتِهِمْ تَأْوِيلُهُ (Rather, they denied what they did not comprehend by knowledge, and whose interpretation has not yet come to them).

**كَذلِكَ كَذَّبَ الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ ** (40) Thus did those before them deny.

[They denied] their prophets (أنبياءهم).

فَانْظُرْ كَيْفَ كانَ عاقِبَةُ الظَّالِمِينَ So observe how was the fate of the wrongdoers!

[This is] a threat (وَعِيد) to them with what those before them were punished.

(٤٠) وَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ يُؤْمِنُ بِهِ ** (40) And among them are those who believe in Him within his soul, and knows that it is true, but he is obstinate, or, and among them are those who believe in Him in the future. وَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ لا يُؤْمِنُ بِهِ ** And among them are those who do not believe in Him within his soul, due to the excess of his foolishness (غَبَاوَتِهِ) and the scarcity of his reflection (تَدَبُّرِهِ), or in what is to come, and persists in disbelief (الكُفْر).

(٤١) Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: They are the enemies of Muhammad and the Family of Muhammad عليهم السلام after him. وَرَبُّكَ أَعْلَمُ بِالْمُفْسِدِينَ (And your Lord is most knowing of the corrupters) refers to the obstinate (الْمُعَانِدِينَ) or the persistent [in sin] (المصرّين).

(٤٢) The Almighty's saying: وَإِنْ كَذَّبُوكَ And if you despair of their response and they persist in denying you,

فَقُلْ لِي عَمَلِي وَلَكُمْ عَمَلُكُمْ أَنْتُمْ بَرِيئُونَ مِمَّا أَعْمَلُ وَأَنَا بَرِيءٌ مِمَّا تَعْمَلُونَ (٦) (But if they deny you, say, 'My deed is for me, and your deed is for you. You are absolved of what I do, and I am absolved of what you do.')

You will not be held accountable for my deed, nor will I be held accountable for your deed. Meaning: declare yourself innocent of them and leave them, for you have presented the excuse (أعذرت) to them. It was said that this [verse] is abrogated (منسوخة) by the verse of fighting (آية القتال).

(٤٣) وَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ يَسْتَمِعُونَ إِلَيْكَ ** when you recite the Quran and teach the laws (الشرايع), but they do not accept, like the deaf person (الأصمِّ) who does not hear. Can you make the deaf hear? أَفَأَنْتَ تُسْمِعُ الصُّمَ (meaning) you are able to make them hear, وَلَوْ كانُوا لا يَعْقِلُونَ ** even if their lack of reason (عدم تعقّلهم) is joined to their deafness (صمَمِهم).

And in this is an alert that the reality of listening to speech is understanding the intended meaning (المعنى المقصود) of it, and for this reason, animals (البهائم) are not described by it. And it does not come about except by using sound intellect (العقل السّليم) in reflecting upon it (تدبّره). And since their intellects were afflicted by opposing illusion (الوهم) and following habit (الإِلفِ) and imitation (التقليد), their understandings (افهامهم) betrayed the wisdom (الحكم) and the subtle meanings (المعاني الدقيقة). Thus, they did not benefit from the recitation (سرد) of the words upon them, except for what the animals (البهائم) benefit from the speech of the caller (النّاعق).

وَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ يَنْظُرُ إِلَيْكَ ** and they witness the signs (دلالات) of your prophethood, but they do not believe. أَفَأَنْتَ تَهْدِي الْعُمْيَ ** (meaning) you are able to guide them, وَلَوْ كانُوا لا يُبْصِرُونَ ** even if the lack of insight (عدم البصيرة) is joined to the lack of sight (عدم البصر). For the purpose of seeing (الإبصار) is taking heed (الإِعتبار) and gaining insight (الإستبصار), and the foundation (العمدة) in that is insight (البصيرة). For this reason, the insightful blind person (الأعمى المستبصر) perceives and understands what the foolish sighted person (البصير الأحمق) does not grasp.

And the Ayah confirms the command to disassociate (التّبري) and turn away (الإعراض) from them.

وَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ يَنْظُرُ إِلَيْكَ أَفَأَنْتَ تَهْدِي الْعُمْيَ وَلَوْ كَانُوا لَا يُبْصِرُونَ (And among them are those who look at you. Can you guide the blind, even if they do not perceive?)

(٤٤) إِنَّ اللهَ لا يَظْلِمُ النَّاسَ شَيْئاً ** (Indeed Allah does not wrong people in the least) He does not diminish anything related to their interests, such as the senses (الحواسّ) and the intellects (العقول). وَلكِنَّ النَّاسَ أَنْفُسَهُمْ يَظْلِمُونَ ** (but it is people who wrong themselves) by corrupting them and causing the loss of their benefits upon themselves. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Indeed, Allah, the Clement (الحليم), the All-Knowing (العليم), His wrath is only upon the one who does not accept His pleasure (رضاه). And He only withholds from the one who does not accept His gift (عطاءه). And He only leads astray the one who does not accept His guidance (هداه). The Hadith.

(٤٥) وَيَوْمَ يَحْشُرُهُمْ ** (45) (And) the day He will muster them And it was read with the yāʾ (بالياءِ). كَأَنْ لَمْ يَلْبَثُوا إِلَّا ساعَةً مِنَ النَّهارِ ** as if they had not stayed (in the world) except for an hour of the day. They deem short the duration of their stay in the world or in the graves (القبور), due to the horror of what they see. يَتَعارَفُونَ بَيْنَهُمْ ** they will recognize one another. Some of them will recognize others, as if they had not been separated except for a short while. It was said that this occurs when they emerge from the graves (القبور), then the mutual recognition (التعارف) ceases due to the severity of the matter upon them. قَدْ خَسِرَ الَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا بِلِقاءِ اللهِ وَما كانُوا مُهْتَدِينَ** Certainly, those who denied the encounter with Allah have lost, and they were not guided.

(٤٦) وَإِمَّا نُرِيَنَّكَ بَعْضَ الَّذِي نَعِدُهُمْ ** (And whether We show you some of what We promise them,) of the punishment in your life, just as He showed it to him on the Day of Badr. And al-Qummi (said it refers) to the Return (الرجعة) and the rise of the Qā'im (peace be upon him). أَوْ نَتَوَفَّيَنَّكَ ** (or We take you away,) before We show it to you. فَإِلَيْنا مَرْجِعُهُمْ ** (their return is to Us.) then We will show it to you in the Hereafter. ثُمَّ اللهُ شَهِيدٌ عَلى ما يَفْعَلُونَ ** (Then Allah is witness to what they do.) He will recompense for it. He mentioned the witnessing (الشهادة) but intended its consequence, and for that reason, He ordered it after the return (الرجوع) using thumma (ثم). Or the intended meaning is that He witnesses their deeds on the Day of Resurrection.

(٤٧) وَلِكُلِّ أُمَّةٍ رَسُولٌ فَإِذا جاءَ رَسُولُهُمْ ** (For every nation there is an apostle. So when their apostle comes) [This means] with clear proofs (بالبيّنات), and they denied him, or on the Day of Resurrection, so that he may bear witness against them, قُضِيَ بَيْنَهُمْ ** (judgment is made between them) [meaning] between the Apostle and those who denied him, بِالْقِسْطِ ** (with justice) [meaning] with justice (بالعدل). So He saved the Apostle and punished the deniers, وَهُمْ لا يُظْلَمُونَ.** (and they will not be wronged.)

In al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Its esoteric interpretation (تفسيرها في الباطن) is that for every generation (قرن) of this nation (الأمّة) there is an apostle (رسولاً) from the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them and their family) who emerges toward the Quran, which is an apostle to them. And they are the Vicegerents (الأولياء) and they are the Apostles (الرّسل). And as for His saying: فَإِذا جاءَ رَسُولُهُمْ قُضِيَ بَيْنَهُمْ بِالْقِسْطِ ** (So when their apostle comes, judgment is made between them with justice), then its meaning is that the Apostles of Allah judge بِالْقِسْطِ وَهُمْ لا يُظْلَمُونَ.** (with justice, and they will not be wronged.)

(٤٨) وَيَقُولُونَ مَتى هذَا الْوَعْدُ ** (48) And they say, 'When will this promise be fulfilled?' (It is) seeking to hasten what they were promised of the punishment, or deeming it far-fetched, إِنْ كُنْتُمْ صادِقِينَ** They share the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and the believers in the address.

(٤٩) قُلْ لا أَمْلِكُ لِنَفْسِي ضَرًّا وَلا نَفْعاً ** (49) Say, 'I have no control over any harm or benefit for myself, So how can I control harm for you? إِلَّا ما شاءَ اللهُ ** (Except) that which He wills that I control, or that which He wills its occurrence, so it occurs. لِكُلِّ أُمَّةٍ أَجَلٌ for their destruction. **إِذا جاءَ أَجَلُهُمْ فَلا يَسْتَأْخِرُونَ ساعَةً وَلا يَسْتَقْدِمُونَ ** When their term comes, they will not defer it by a single hour, nor advance it. When that fixed term comes, He will fulfill His promise to you.

Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It (the fixed term) is that which is named for the Angel of Death (ملك الموت) on the Night of Decree (ليلة القدر).

(٥٠) قُلْ أَرَأَيْتُمْ Inform me إِنْ أَتاكُمْ عَذابُهُ which you hasten بَياتاً at the time of resting (بيات) and being occupied with sleep أَوْ نَهاراً when you were occupied seeking your livelihood ما ذا يَسْتَعْجِلُ مِنْهُ الْمُجْرِمُونَ Which thing of the punishment do they seek to hasten? And nothing of it necessitates hastening. He placed الْمُجْرِمُونَ in the place of the pronoun to indicate that, due to their crime (لجرمهم), they ought to fear the coming of the threat, not seek to hasten it. Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): This is a punishment that descends in the End of Time upon the wicked (فسقة) among the People of the Qiblah (أهل القبلة), while they deny the descent of the punishment upon them. And in al-Majma', narrated from him (peace be upon him): That which is similar in meaning.

(٥١) **أَثُمَّ إِذا ما وَقَعَ آمَنْتُمْ بِهِ ** (51) "Is it then, when it has occurred, that you will believe in it?"

After its occurrence, when belief in it will not benefit you. "Now?" (آلْآنَ) is on the intention of saying (على إرادة القول), meaning: it was said to them when they believed after the occurrence of the punishment, "Now have you believed?" وَقَدْ كُنْتُمْ بِهِ تَسْتَعْجِلُونَ (while you used to seek to hasten it) as a denial (تكذيباً) and mockery (واستهزاءً).

(٥٢) ثُمَّ قِيلَ لِلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا ذُوقُوا عَذابَ الْخُلْدِ هَلْ تُجْزَوْنَ إِلَّا بِما كُنْتُمْ تَكْسِبُونَ ** (52) Then it will be said to those who did wrong, 'Taste the everlasting punishment! Are you being requited for anything but what you used to earn?' [This refers to] disbelief (الكفر) and acts of disobedience (المعاصي). (٥٣) وَيَسْتَنْبِئُونَكَ ** And they seek news from you (ويستخبرونك) أَحَقٌّ هُوَ ** Is what you say true (أحقّ ما تقول), concerning the promise [of reward] (الوعد) and the threat [of punishment] (الوعيد) and other than that (1)? And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "What do you say concerning Ali (peace be upon him)?" And in al-Majalis, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "The people of Mecca seek news from you concerning Ali—is he an Imam?" And al-Qummi narrated similarly: قُلْ إِي وَرَبِّي إِنَّهُ لَحَقٌ (Say, 'Yes, by my Lord! It is surely the truth!) وَما أَنْتُمْ بِمُعْجِزِينَ ** [Meaning:] escaping Him (فايتين إيّاه).

(٥٤) وَلَوْ أَنَّ لِكُلِّ نَفْسٍ ظَلَمَتْ ما فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (54) If every soul that committed wrong had all that is on the earth, of its treasures and its wealth, لَافْتَدَتْ بِهِ ** it would make it a ransom for itself from the punishment. **وَأَسَرُّوا النَّدامَةَ لَمَّا رَأَوُا الْعَذابَ ** They will conceal their regret when they see the punishment, because they were stunned by what they witnessed of the horror and terror of the matter, which they had not anticipated.

In al-Qummi: Regarding ظَلَمَتْ ** (committed wrong), he means the family of Muhammad (prayers of Allah be upon them and their family) regarding their right (حقّهم). Regarding لَافْتَدَتْ بِهِ ** (it would surely offer it as a ransom), he means during the Return (الرّجعة).

In al-Majma', al-Qummi, and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked what benefit there is for them in concealing their regret (إسرار الندامة) while they are in the punishment. He said: "They disliked the gloating (شماتة) of the enemies (الأعداءِ)."

وَقُضِيَ بَيْنَهُمْ بِالْقِسْطِ ** and judgment will be made between them justly, between the wrongdoers (الظّالِمين) and the wronged (المظلومين), and they will not be wronged.

(٥٥) أَلا إِنَّ لِلَّهِ ما فِي السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ** (55) Indeed, all that is in the heavens and the earth belongs to Allah. A confirmation of His power (Exalted is He) over reward and punishment. أَلا إِنَّ وَعْدَ اللهِ حَقٌ ** Indeed, Allah’s promise is true. There is no failing in it. وَلكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ لا يَعْلَمُونَ ** but most of them do not know.** Because their knowledge does not exceed the apparent aspect of the worldly life.

footnote (١) وهذا الاستخبار منهم يحتمل أن يكون انّما وقع منهم على وجه التّعريف والاستفهام ويحتمل أن يكون وقع على وجه الاستهزاء م ن. (1) And this inquiry from them is likely to have occurred by way of seeking knowledge and questioning, and it is also likely to have occurred by way of mockery. م ن.

(٥٦) هُوَ يُحيِي وَيُمِيتُ وَإِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ. (56) He revives and He kills, and to Him you shall be brought back. (٥٧) يا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ قَدْ جاءَتْكُمْ مَوْعِظَةٌ مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ وَشِفاءٌ لِما فِي الصُّدُورِ وَهُدىً وَرَحْمَةٌ لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ ** (57) O mankind! There has come to you an exhortation from your Lord, and a cure for what is in the breasts, and a guidance and a mercy for the faithful. Meaning, a Book (كتاب) encompassing these benefits has come to you. In al-Ihlilijah, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): that it is شِفاءٌ ** (a cure) from the ailments of the thoughts (الخواطر) and the ambiguities of affairs (مشتبهات الأمور). And in al-Kafi, in the Hadith (الحديث) al-Qudsi (القدسي): [It is a cure] from the insufflation (نفث) of Satan (1). And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), from his father, from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace): that a man complained to him of a pain in his chest. So he said: "Seek cure (استشف) through the Quran, for Allah says: وَشِفاءٌ لِما فِي الصُّدُورِ (and a cure for what is in the breasts)." Al-Qummi said, after mentioning the Ayah: The Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) said: "And the Quran."

(٥٨) قُلْ بِفَضْلِ اللهِ وَبِرَحْمَتِهِ فَبِذلِكَ فَلْيَفْرَحُوا ** (58) Say: 'In Allah's grace and in His mercy—in that let them rejoice.' Meaning, if they rejoice in anything, let them rejoice in these two (His grace and His mercy). هُوَ خَيْرٌ مِمَّا يَجْمَعُونَ (That is better than what they accumulate) of the debris (حطام) of the world. And it was recited with the tāʾ (بالتاءِ).

In al-Majma' and al-Jawami', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The grace (فضل) of Allah is the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and His mercy (رحمته) is 'Ali ibn Abi Talib.

And al-Qummi added: فَبِذلِكَ ** (In that) let our followers (شيعتنا) rejoice. That is better than what our enemies were given of gold and silver.**

And al-'Ayyashi narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): That which is similar in meaning.

And in al-Majalis, narrated from the Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم: The grace (فضل) of Allah is the prophethood (نبوّة) of your Prophet, and His mercy (رحمته) is the guardianship (ولاية) of 'Ali ibn Abi Talib. فَبِذلِكَ ** He said: By the prophethood (بالنبوّة) and the guardianship (والولاية), فَلْيَفْرَحُوا ** meaning the followers (الشيعة). هُوَ خَيْرٌ مِمَّا يَجْمَعُونَ ** meaning their opponents (مخالفهم) accumulate of family, wealth, and children in the abode of the world (دار الدّنيا).

And al-'Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): That which is close to it.

footnote (١) النّفث شبيه بالنّفخ وهو أقلّ من التّفل لأنّ التفل لا يكون الّا ومعه شيء من الرّيق والنّفث نفخ لطيف بلا ريق وفي الدعاء وأعوذ بك من نفث الشّيطان وهو ما يلقيه في قلب الإنسان ويوقعه في باله ممّا يصطاده به ونفث الشّيطان على لسانه اي القى فتكلّم ومن هذا لم يزل الإمام مدفوعاً عنه نفوث كلّ غاسق م. (1) Al-Nafth (النّفث) is similar to blowing (النّفخ), and it is less than spitting (التّفل). This is because spitting (التفل) does not occur except with some saliva (الرّيق), whereas al-Nafth (النّفث) is a gentle blow without saliva (بلا ريق). And in the supplication (الدعاء): "And I seek refuge in You from the nafth (نفث) of Satan," which is what he casts into the heart of man and instills in his mind, by which he hunts him. And the nafth (نفث) of Satan upon his tongue means he cast (something) so he spoke. And due to this, the Imam (الإمام) is constantly repelled from the nufūth (نفوث) of every darkener (غاسق).

(٥٩) قُلْ أَرَأَيْتُمْ ** Inform me ما أَنْزَلَ اللهُ لَكُمْ مِنْ رِزْقٍ ** all of it being lawful (حلال) فَجَعَلْتُمْ مِنْهُ حَراماً وَحَلالاً so you made some of it unlawful (حراماً) and some of it lawful (حلالاً).

قُلْ أَرَأَيْتُمْ ما أَنْزَلَ اللهُ لَكُمْ مِنْ رِزْقٍ فَجَعَلْتُمْ مِنْهُ حَراماً وَحَلالاً قُلْ آللهُ أَذِنَ لَكُمْ أَمْ عَلَى اللهِ تَفْتَرُونَ (Say, 'Tell me, what provision Allah has sent down for you, and you have made some of it unlawful and some lawful.' Say, 'Has Allah permitted you, or do you fabricate lies against Allah?')

such as [your saying]: هذِهِ أَنْعامٌ وَحَرْثٌ حِجْرٌ ما فِي بُطُونِ هذِهِ الْأَنْعامِ خالِصَةٌ لِذُكُورِنا وَمُحَرَّمٌ عَلى أَزْواجِنا (These are cattle and crops forbidden; what is in the bellies of these cattle is reserved for our males and forbidden to our wives).

قُلْ آللهُ أَذِنَ لَكُمْ regarding the declaring unlawful (التحريم) and the declaring lawful (التّحليل)—so they say that is by His ruling— أَمْ عَلَى اللهِ تَفْتَرُونَ regarding the attribution of that to Him.

(٦٠) وَما ظَنُّ الَّذِينَ يَفْتَرُونَ عَلَى اللهِ الْكَذِبَ ** (60) What is the thought of those who fabricate lies against Allah What is their thought (ظنّهم) يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ ** on the Day of Resurrection? Do they reckon that they will not escape it (يجاوزوا عليه)? And this is a great threat, since He has left the matter ambiguous (أبهم الأمر). إِنَّ اللهَ لَذُو فَضْلٍ عَلَى النَّاسِ ** Indeed, Allah is gracious to mankind, due to what He has done for them in terms of various kinds of blessings (ضروب الإنعام). وَلكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ لا يَشْكُرُونَ ** but most of them are not grateful. for His blessings (نعمه).

(٦١) وَما تَكُونُ ** O Muhammad فِي شَأْنٍ ** in any matter (فِي أَمْر) وَما تَتْلُوا مِنْهُ ** from that affair (مِنَ الشَّأْن) مِنْ قُرْآنٍ وَلا تَعْمَلُونَ ** all of you (أَنْتُمْ جَمِيعاً) مِنْ عَمَلٍ إِلَّا كُنَّا عَلَيْكُمْ شُهُوداً إِذْ تُفِيضُونَ فِيهِ ** you delve into it (تَخُوضُونَ فِيهِ) and rush into it (تَنْدَفِعُونَ).

وَمَا تَكُونُ فِي شَأْنٍ وَمَا تَتْلُو مِنْهُ مِن قُرْآنٍ وَلَا تَعْمَلُونَ مِنْ عَمَلٍ إِلَّا كُنَّا عَلَيْكُمْ شُهُودًا إِذْ تُفِيضُونَ فِيهِ ۚ وَمَا يَعْزُبُ عَن رَّبِّكَ مِن مِّثْقَالِ ذَرَّةٍ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَلَا فِي السَّمَاءِ وَلَا أَصْغَرَ مِن ذَٰلِكَ وَلَا أَكْبَرَ إِلَّا فِي كِتَابٍ مُّبِينٍ (61) You are not engaged in any affair, nor do you recite any part of the Quran from it, nor do you perform any deed, but that We are witnesses over you when you are engrossed in it. Nor does anything escape your Lord’s knowledge, even the weight of a speck of dust in the earth or in the sky, nor anything smaller than that, nor anything greater, but it is in an obvious Book.

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) and al-Qummi, he said: When this verse was recited, the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم would weep intensely. وَما يَعْزُبُ عَنْ رَبِّكَ ** nor does anything become distant (يَبْعُد) or absent (يَغِيب) from His knowledge. And it was recited with the kasrah on the zāy (بِكَسْرِ الزَّاي). مِنْ مِثْقالِ ذَرَّةٍ ** what weighs as much as a small ant (نَمْلَة صَغِيرَة) or motes (هَبَاء) فِي الْأَرْضِ وَلا فِي السَّماءِ وَلا أَصْغَرَ مِنْ ذلِكَ وَلا أَكْبَرَ إِلَّا فِي كِتابٍ مُبِينٍ . This is a new clause (اسْتِينَاف) confirming what preceded it. And it was recited with the raf' (بِالرَّفْعِ) in both (words).

(٦٢) أَلا إِنَّ أَوْلِياءَ اللهِ لا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ ** (62) Lo! Indeed, the friends of Allah will have no fear (of any harm befalling them) وَلا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ ** (nor will they grieve) (over the loss of any desired thing). (٦٣) الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَكانُوا يَتَّقُونَ ** (63) those who have faith and are Godwary. (This is) an explanation of the friends of Allah (أولياء الله), or it is a new sentence whose predicate is what follows it.

In al-Ayyashi, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "They are us and our followers, those who follow us after us. Blessedness (طوبى) is for us, and blessedness (طوبى) is for them. And their blessedness (طوباهم) is superior to our blessedness (طوبانا)." It was asked: "Why is their blessedness (طوباهم) superior to our blessedness (طوبانا)? Are we not, both we and they, upon the same matter (أمر)?" He said: "No. Indeed, they were burdened with what you were not burdened with, and they endured what you could not endure."

And in al-Ikmal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Blessedness (طوبى) is for the followers (شيعة) of our Qā’im (قائمنا), those who await His appearance during His occultation (غيبته) and are obedient to Him during His appearance. Those are the friends of Allah (62) who will have no fear nor will they grieve."

And in al-Jawami', narrated from the Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم: He was asked about the friends of Allah (أولياء الله), and He said: "They are those who remind people of Allah when they are seen," meaning, in their demeanor (السّمت) and appearance (الهيئة).

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Whoever knows Allah and glorifies Him, restrains his mouth from speech and his stomach from food, and burdens his soul with fasting (الصيام) and standing (in prayer) (القيام)." They said: "May our fathers and mothers be sacrificed for you, O Messenger of Allah, are these the friends of Allah (أولياء الله)?" He said: "Indeed, the friends of Allah (أولياء الله) remained silent, so their silence was remembrance (ذكراً); they looked, so their looking was a lesson (عبرة); they spoke, so their speech was wisdom (حكمة); and they walked, so their walking among the people was a blessing (بركة). Were it not for the appointed times (الآجال) decreed for them, their souls would not remain settled in their bodies, due to fear of the punishment and longing for the reward."

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: "We found in the book of Ali ibn al-Husayn (peace be upon him): Lo! Indeed, the friends of Allah will have no fear nor will they grieve (62) when they fulfill the obligation (فرض) of Allah, adhere to the practices (سنن) of the Messenger of Allah صلَّى الله عليه وآله وسلم, abstain from the prohibitions (محارم) of Allah, renounce the immediate splendor (زهرة) of the world, desire what is with Allah, and earn the pure (الطيّب) of the provision (رزق) of Allah, not desiring boastfulness (التفاخر) or accumulation (التكاثر). Then they spend what is incumbent upon them from obligatory rights (حقوق واجبة). Those are the ones whom Allah has blessed in what they have earned, and they will be rewarded for what they have sent forth for their Hereafter." And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): The like of it.

(64) لَهُمُ الْبُشْرى فِي الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا وَفِي الْآخِرَةِ. (For them is good news in the life of the world and in the Hereafter.) In al-Kafi and al-Faqih, narrated from the Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم, and in al-Qummi: الْبُشْرى فِي الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا ** (good news in the life of the world) is the good dream (الرّؤيا الحَسَنة) that the believer sees, and through which he is given good tidings in his worldly life. And it is added in al-Faqih: As for His saying فِي الْآخِرَةِ ** (in the Hereafter), it is the good tidings given to the believer at the time of death. He is given good tidings at his death that Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, has forgiven you and those who carry you to your grave. And in al-Qummi: وَفِي الْآخِرَةِ ** (and in the Hereafter) is at the time of death, and it is His Almighty saying: الَّذِينَ تَتَوَفَّاهُمُ الْمَلائِكَةُ طَيِّبِينَ (those whom the angels take away while they are pure) يَقُولُونَ سَلامٌ عَلَيْكُمْ ادْخُلُوا الْجَنَّةَ. (saying, 'Peace be upon you! Enter paradise.') And in al-Jawami', narrated from the Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم: It is فِي الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا ** (in the life of the world) the righteous dream (الرؤيا الصالحة) that the believer sees for himself or is seen for him, and وَفِي الْآخِرَةِ (and in the Hereafter) is Paradise. And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: Regarding this Ayah, He gives them good tidings of the rising of the Qā’im (القائم), of his appearance, of the killing of their enemies, of salvation in the Hereafter, and of arriving at the Basin (الحوض) with Muhammad and his truthful Progeny. And narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: When a man's soul reaches his chest, he sees the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم, who says to him: "I am the Messenger of Allah, receive good tidings." Then he sees Ali ibn Abi Talib عليه السلام, who says to him: "I am Ali ibn Abi Talib, whom you used to love. I shall benefit you today." He said: And that is in the Quran, His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَكانُوا يَتَّقُونَ لَهُمُ الْبُشْرى فِي الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا وَفِي الْآخِرَةِ. (those who have faith and are Godwary. For them is good news in the life of the world and in the Hereafter.) And in it (al-Kafi) and in al-Ayyashi, there are other narrations (أخبار أُخر) concerning its meaning. And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: When the soul of one of you reaches here [pointing to the throat], the Angel of Death (ملك الموت) descends upon him and says to him: "As for what you hoped for, you have been granted it, and as for what you feared, you are safe from it." And a door is opened for him to his abode in Paradise, and it is said to him: "Look at your dwelling in Paradise, and look, this is the Messenger of Allah, and the Commander of the Faithful (أمير المؤمنين), and al-Hasan and al-Husayn (may the blessings of Allah be upon them) are your companions." And that is the saying of Allah, the Blessed and Exalted and Sanctified: الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَكانُوا يَتَّقُونَ ** (those who have faith and are Godwary) [the rest of] the Ayah. لا تَبْدِيلَ لِكَلِماتِ اللهِ ** (There is no changing the words of Allah). There is no alteration to His sayings, nor any failure in His promises. And this is an interjection (اعتراض). ذلِكَ ** (That) [which is] a reference to their being given good tidings in both abodes هُوَ الْفَوْزُ الْعَظِيمُ.** (is the great success.)

(٦٥) وَلا يَحْزُنْكَ قَوْلُهُمْ ** (And let not their saying grieve you) [Meaning] their denial, their plotting to invalidate your affair, and all the rest of what they speak concerning your matter إِنَّ الْعِزَّةَ لِلَّهِ جَمِيعاً ** (Indeed, all might belongs entirely to Allah) Indeed, the subjugation (القهر) and the dominance (الغلبة) entirely belong to Allah; no one possesses anything of the two besides Him. So He will dominate them and grant you victory over them إِنَّا لَنَنْصُرُ رُسُلَنا هُوَ السَّمِيعُ ** (Indeed, We will surely help Our messengers. He is the All-hearing) of what they say, the All-knowing of what they resolve (يعزمون). So He will recompense them for that.

(66) أَلا إِنَّ لِلَّهِ مَنْ فِي السَّماواتِ وَمَنْ فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (Look! Indeed belongs to Allah whoever is in the heavens and whoever is on the earth.) (This refers to) the angels and the two weighty ones (الثقلين). And since these are His servants, and they are within His dominion, none of them is fit for divinity (للإِلهية), despite their being rational and discerning (عقلاء مميّزون). Then that which neither discerns nor reasons is even more deserving of not being a partner to Him. وَما يَتَّبِعُ الَّذِينَ يَدْعُونَ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ شُرَكاءَ ** (And those who invoke besides Allah partners do not follow [them].) (The word) شُرَكاءَ (partners) is used restrictively for one of them (the two objects of following), meaning: partners in reality, even if they call them partners. Or the meaning is: And they do not follow certainty (يقيناً), which was omitted because what follows indicates it: إِنْ يَتَّبِعُونَ إِلَّا الظَّنَ (They follow nothing but conjecture) except their conjecture (ظنّهم) that they are partners. وَإِنْ هُمْ إِلَّا يَخْرُصُونَ ** (and they are only guessing.) They estimate a false estimation (تقديراً باطلاً). And it is permissible that ما (mā) is interrogative (استفهاميّة), meaning: "And what thing do they follow?" Or it is relative (موصولة), being conjoined to مَنْ (man), meaning: "And to Allah belongs that which they follow."

(67) هُوَ الَّذِي جَعَلَ لَكُمُ اللَّيْلَ لِتَسْكُنُوا فِيهِ وَالنَّهارَ مُبْصِراً ** (It is He who made the night for you, that you may rest in it, and the day sight-giving.) (This is) an alert concerning the perfection of His power and the greatness of His favor, so that He may guide them to His uniqueness in deserving worship. إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآياتٍ لِقَوْمٍ يَسْمَعُونَ ** (There are indeed signs in that for a people who listen.) listening of reflection and understanding.

(٦٨) قالُوا اتَّخَذَ اللهُ وَلَداً ** (68) They say, 'Allah has taken a son.' meaning a daughter. سُبْحانَهُ ** Immaculate is He! A declaration of His transcendence (تنزيه) and astonishment (تعجّب) at their foolish statement. هُوَ الْغَنِيُ ** He is the All-sufficient. He does not need to take a son. لَهُ ما فِي السَّماواتِ وَما فِي الْأَرْضِ ** To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth. A confirmation of His self-sufficiency (غناه). إِنْ عِنْدَكُمْ مِنْ سُلْطانٍ بِهذا Do you have any authority for this? You do not have any proof (حجّة) for this saying. أَتَقُولُونَ عَلَى اللهِ ما لا تَعْلَمُونَ ** Do you ascribe to Allah what you do not know? A reprimand (توبيخ) and censure (تقريع) for their disagreement (اختلافهم) and their ignorance (جهلهم). When He negated the proof (الحجّة) from them, He rendered them ignorant (غير عالمين). This indicates that every statement (قول) for which there is no evidence (برهان) is ignorance (جهل), not knowledge (علم).

(٦٩) قُلْ إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَفْتَرُونَ عَلَى اللهِ الْكَذِبَ ** (69) Say, 'Indeed those who fabricate lies against Allah' (by taking a son and attributing a partner to Him) لا يُفْلِحُونَ (they will not prosper/succeed); they will not escape the Fire, nor will they attain the Garden (الجنّة). (٧٠) مَتاعٌ فِي الدُّنْيا ** (70) is an enjoyment in the world. Their fabrication is a slight enjoyment in the world, by which they establish their leadership in disbelief (الكفر). ثُمَّ إِلَيْنا مَرْجِعُهُمْ ** Then to Us will be their return. (by death, and they will meet eternal misery). ثُمَّ نُذِيقُهُمُ الْعَذابَ الشَّدِيدَ بِما كانُوا يَكْفُرُونَ ** Then We shall make them taste the severe punishment because of their being faithless. due to their disbelief (كفُرهم).

(٧١) وَاتْلُ عَلَيْهِمْ نَبَأَ نُوحٍ إِذْ قالَ لِقَوْمِهِ يا قَوْمِ إِنْ كانَ كَبُرَ ** (71) Recite to them the account of Noah, when he said to his people, 'O my people! If my stay [among you] and my reminding you of Allah's signs is hard on you, became great (عظم) and difficult (وشقّ) for you مَقامِي my standing—my place (مكاني) and my residence (واقامتي) among you for a long period (مدّة مديدة), or my persistence (قيامي) in the call (على الدّعوة) وَتَذْكِيرِي ** your reminding (إيّاكم) بِآياتِ اللهِ فَعَلَى اللهِ تَوَكَّلْتُ ** then in Him (فبه) I trust (وثقت) فَأَجْمِعُوا أَمْرَكُمْ so resolve upon what you desire (ما تريدون) وَشُرَكَاءَكُمْ ** along with your partners (مع شركائكم) and gather together to strive for my destruction (في اهلاكي) ثُمَّ لا يَكُنْ أَمْرُكُمْ عَلَيْكُمْ غُمَّةً ** concealed (مستوراً), but make it manifest (ظاهراً) and uncovered (مكشوفاً), derived from ghammuhu (غمّه) when he concealed (ستره) it. And al-Qummi (said): Do not be distressed (لا تغتمّوا). ثُمَّ اقْضُوا إِلَيَ then carry out (أدّوا إلىَ) that matter (ذلك الأمر) which you desire for me. And al-Qummi (said): Then invoke [evil] against me (ادعوا عليّ) وَلا تُنْظِرُونِ ** and do not grant me respite (ولا تمهلوني).

(٧٢) فَإِنْ تَوَلَّيْتُمْ ** (Then if you turn away) You turn away (أَعْرَضْتُمْ) from my reminder (تَذْكِيرِي). فَما سَأَلْتُكُمْ مِنْ أَجْرٍ ** (I have not asked you any reward) which necessitates your turning away due to its burden upon you, and your accusing me because of it. إِنْ أَجْرِيَ ** (My reward is not) What is my reward (ثَوَابِي) for the call (الدَّعْوَةِ) and the reminder (التَّذْكِيرِ). إِلَّا عَلَى اللهِ ** (except with Allah) It has no connection to you by which He rewards me, whether you believe or turn away. وَأُمِرْتُ أَنْ أَكُونَ مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ (and I have been commanded to be one of the submitters.) those who submit (الْمُنْقَادِينَ) to His judgment (حُكْمِهِ); I do not disobey His command, nor do I hope for other than Him.

(٧٣) فَكَذَّبُوهُ ** (73) But they impugned him. So they persisted in denying him after he had established the proof against them, and their denial of him at the end of the long period was just like their denial at its beginning. فَنَجَّيْناهُ وَمَنْ مَعَهُ فِي الْفُلْكِ ** Then We delivered him and those who were with him in the Ark, from drowning. وَجَعَلْناهُمْ خَلائِفَ ** and We made them successors, successors (خلفاء) to those who perished by drowning. وَأَغْرَقْنَا الَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا بِآياتِنا ** and We drowned those who impugned Our signs. by the flood (الطّوفان). **فَانْظُرْ كَيْفَ كانَ عاقِبَةُ الْمُنْذَرِينَ ** So observe how was the fate of those who were warned! (This is) a magnification of what befell them, and a warning to whoever denies the Messenger against the like of it, and a consolation for him (the Messenger).

(٧٤) ثُمَّ بَعَثْنا مِنْ بَعْدِهِ ** (Then We raised up after him) We sent after Noah رُسُلاً إِلى قَوْمِهِمْ ** (apostles to their peoples) meaning Hud (هوداً), Salih (صالحاً), Ibrahim (إبراهيم), Lut (لوطاً), and Shu'ayb (شعيباً), each one to his people. فَجاؤُهُمْ بِالْبَيِّناتِ ** (and they brought them manifest proofs) with clear miracles (المعجزات الواضحة) confirming their claim. فَما كانُوا لِيُؤْمِنُوا (but they would not believe) so it was not fitting for them to believe, due to the severity of their persistence (تصممهم) (1) upon disbelief بِما كَذَّبُوا بِهِ مِنْ قَبْلُ (because of what they had denied before.) meaning in the realm of the seed (الذّر). The narrations concerning this meaning have already passed in Surat al-A'raf. كَذلِكَ نَطْبَعُ عَلى قُلُوبِ الْمُعْتَدِينَ ** (Thus do We set a seal on the hearts of the transgressors.) with abandonment (بالخذلان), due to their immersion (لانهماكهم) in error and following what is customary (المألوف).

(٧٥) **ثُمَّ بَعَثْنا مِنْ بَعْدِهِمْ مُوسى وَهارُونَ إِلى فِرْعَوْنَ وَمَلَائِهِ بِآياتِنا فَاسْتَكْبَرُوا وَكانُوا قَوْماً مُجْرِمِينَ ** (75) Then We sent after them Moses and Aaron to Pharaoh and his elite with Our signs, but they acted arrogantly and they were a guilty people.

after these Messengers, Moses and Aaron to Pharaoh and his faction, with Our signs, meaning with the nine signs. But they acted arrogantly from following the two of them, and they were a guilty people, habitually committing the crime (الإجرام). For that reason, they treated the message of their Lord lightly and dared to reject it.

(٧٦) فَلَمَّا جاءَهُمُ الْحَقُّ مِنْ عِنْدِنا ** (76) But when the truth came to them from Us, And they recognized it through the manifestation of overwhelming miracles that dispel (المزيحة) doubt, they said, out of the excess of their rebellion (تمرّدهم): إِنَّ هذا لَسِحْرٌ مُبِينٌ ** 'This is indeed manifest magic.' — meaning, obvious (ظاهِر).

(٧٧) **قالَ مُوسى أَتَقُولُونَ لِلْحَقِّ لَمَّا جاءَكُمْ ** (77) Moses said, 'Do you say [this] about the truth when it has come to you?

"It is indeed sorcery." The narrated statement was omitted because what precedes it and what follows it indicate it. Or the meaning is: Do you find fault with the truth and impugn it? أَسِحْرٌ هذا ** [This is] a resumption (of speech) by denying what they said.
