It is not part of the narrated statement because they affirmed (1) the statement. وَلا يُفْلِحُ السَّاحِرُونَ ** [This is] from the completion of the speech of Moses.

(٧٨) قالُوا أَجِئْتَنا لِتَلْفِتَنا ** (78) They said, 'Have you come to us to turn us away To turn us away عَمَّا وَجَدْنا عَلَيْهِ آباءَنا ** from what we found our fathers following, from the worship of idols, وَتَكُونَ لَكُمَا الْكِبْرِياءُ فِي الْأَرْضِ and that the supremacy in the land may belong to you two? meaning, the dominion therein, because kings are characterized by greatness (الكبر). **وَما نَحْنُ لَكُما بِمُؤْمِنِينَ ** We are not going to believe you two.' believing in what you have brought.

(٧٩) وَقالَ فِرْعَوْنُ ائْتُونِي بِكُلِّ ساحِرٍ عَلِيمٍ ** (79) 'Bring me every skilled magician.' [Meaning] expert (حاذق) in it. And it was recited (سحار). (٨٠) فَلَمَّا جاءَ السَّحَرَةُ قالَ لَهُمْ مُوسى أَلْقُوا ما أَنْتُمْ مُلْقُونَ. (80) When the magicians came, Moses said to them, 'Cast what you have to cast.' (٨١) فَلَمَّا أَلْقَوْا قالَ مُوسى ما جِئْتُمْ بِهِ السِّحْرُ ** (81) When they had cast, Moses said, 'What you have brought is sorcery. Allah will surely nullify it. Indeed Allah does not set right the work of the agents of corruption.' [Meaning] that which you brought, not what you named sorcery. And it was recited (آلسّحر) with the alif separated and lengthened, indicating interrogation (الاستفهام), so ما is interrogative (استفهاميّة). إِنَّ اللهَ سَيُبْطِلُهُ ** He will destroy it and reveal its falsehood. إِنَّ اللهَ لا يُصْلِحُ عَمَلَ الْمُفْسِدِينَ ** He does not establish it nor strengthen it.

(٨٢) وَيُحِقُّ اللهُ الْحَقَ He establishes it بِكَلِماتِهِ by His commands and His decrees وَلَوْ كَرِهَ الْمُجْرِمُونَ. (82) And Allah confirms the truth with His words, though the guilty should be averse. (٨٣) فَما آمَنَ لِمُوسى at the beginning of his affair إِلَّا ذُرِّيَّةٌ مِنْ قَوْمِهِ except the children/offspring from the people of Moses, meaning the Children of Israel (بني إسرائيل), or the people of Pharaoh. It is said: He called them, but they did not respond to him out of fear of Pharaoh, except a group of their youths (شبانهم) عَلى خَوْفٍ مِنْ فِرْعَوْنَ وَمَلَائِهِمْ meaning the party/faction of Pharaoh's folk أَنْ يَفْتِنَهُمْ that Pharaoh might torture them وَإِنَّ فِرْعَوْنَ لَعالٍ فِي الْأَرْضِ He was dominant/overpowering in it وَإِنَّهُ لَمِنَ الْمُسْرِفِينَ in arrogance (الكبر), obstinacy (العتّو), injustice (الظلم), and corruption (الفساد), until he claimed Lordship (الرّبوبيّة) and enslaved the descendants (أسباط) of the Prophets. (83) But none believed in Moses except a few of his people’s offspring, for fear of Pharaoh and his elite, lest he should persecute them. Indeed, Pharaoh was high and mighty in the land, and indeed, he was one of the transgressors.

(٨٤) وَقالَ مُوسى ** when he saw the fear of those who believed in him: يا قَوْمِ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ آمَنْتُمْ بِاللهِ فَعَلَيْهِ تَوَكَّلُوا ** (My people! If you have believed in Allah, then put your trust in Him, if you are Muslims.)

So trust in Him, and entrust your affair to Him, and rely upon Him. إِنْ كُنْتُمْ مُسْلِمِينَ ** (if you are Muslims)—submitting to the decree of Allah, sincere to Him. And this is not the conditioning of the ruling upon two conditions, for what is conditioned upon faith (الإيمان) is the obligation of reliance (وجوب التوكّل), for it is what necessitates it (المقتضي له). And what is conditioned upon Islam (الإسلام) is its realization (حصوله), for it is not found alongside confusion (التخليط). Its parallel is: If Zayd calls you, then answer him if you are able.

footnote (١) *Al-Batt* (*البتّ*) means cutting (*القطع*), that is, they affirmed it (*جزموا به*). M.

(85) فَقالُوا عَلَى اللهِ تَوَكَّلْنا ** (Then they said: 'In Allah we have put our trust.') because they were sincere believers, and for that reason, their supplication was answered. 'Our Lord! Do not make us a trial (موضع فتنة - place of trial) لِلْقَوْمِ الظَّالِمِينَ ** (for the wrongdoing lot),' meaning: Do not give them dominance over us, lest they tempt us away from our religion or torture us.

In al-Majma', narrated from both of them (peace be upon them both), and in al-Ayyashi in a truncated form (مقطوعاً): Do not give them dominance over us, lest You tempt them through us.

And al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Indeed, the people of Moses were enslaved by the family of Pharaoh, and [Pharaoh's people] said: "If these people had honor (كرامة) as they claim, we would not have been given dominance over them."

وَقالَ مُوسى ** (And Moses said) to his people: يا قَوْمِ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ آمَنْتُمْ ** ('O my people! If you have faith') (the rest of the verse).

I say: This narration is an explanation of the first narration.

(٨٦) **وَنَجِّنا بِرَحْمَتِكَ مِنَ الْقَوْمِ الْكافِرِينَ ** (86) and deliver us by Your mercy from the faithless lot.’ [Deliver us] from their plotting and their enslavement of us.

(٨٧) وَأَوْحَيْنا إِلى مُوسى وَأَخِيهِ أَنْ تَبَوَّءا لِقَوْمِكُما بِمِصْرَ بُيُوتاً وَاجْعَلُوا بُيُوتَكُمْ قِبْلَةً وَأَقِيمُوا الصَّلاةَ ** (87) We revealed to Moses and his brother: ‘Settle your people in houses in Egypt, and make your houses a qiblah, and maintain the prayer.’ أَنْ تَبَوَّءا ** means: take a dwelling place (مَبَاءَة), that is, a place of return (مَرْجَعًا). لِقَوْمِكُما بِمِصْرَ بُيُوتاً ** [means] houses which you return to for worship. And make—you two and your people—بُيُوتَكُمْ —those houses—a qiblah (قِبْلَةً), a place of prayer (مُصَلَّى). وَأَقِيمُوا الصَّلاةَ ** [means] in them.

In al-Qummi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): When the Children of Israel feared their tyrants, Allah revealed to Moses and Aaron: أَنْ تَبَوَّءا لِقَوْمِكُما بِمِصْرَ بُيُوتاً وَاجْعَلُوا بُيُوتَكُمْ قِبْلَةً (Settle your people in houses in Egypt, and make your houses a qiblah). He said: They were commanded to pray in their houses. **وَبَشِّرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ** (and give good news to the faithful) [means] with victory in this world and Paradise in the Hereafter.

In al-Ilal and al-Ayyashi: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم addressed the people and said: "O people, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, commanded Moses and Aaron to build لِقَوْمِكُما بِمِصْرَ بُيُوتاً ** (houses for your people in Egypt), and He commanded them that no one in a state of major ritual impurity (جُنُب) should spend the night in their mosque, nor should women approach it, except for Aaron and his progeny. And verily, Ali is to me in the position of Aaron to Moses. Thus, it is not lawful for anyone to approach women in my mosque, nor to spend the night in it while in a state of major ritual impurity (جُنُب), except for Ali and his progeny. So, whoever is displeased by that, let him go here," and He struck His hand towards Syria (الشّام).

And in al-Uyun: [A narration] close to it.

(٨٨) وَقالَ مُوسى رَبَّنا إِنَّكَ آتَيْتَ فِرْعَوْنَ وَمَلَأَهُ زِينَةً ** (88) (Moses said, 'Our Lord! You have indeed given Pharaoh and his elite adornment) That by which they adorn themselves, such as clothing (اللّباس), furnishings (الفرش), mounts (المراكب), and the like. وَأَمْوالاً ** (and wealth) And various kinds of wealth (المال). فِي الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا رَبَّنا لِيُضِلُّوا عَنْ سَبِيلِكَ. (in the life of the world, Our Lord, that they may lead [people] astray from Your way.) Al-Qummi (said): Meaning, that they may tempt (يفتنوا) people with wealth (الأموال) so that they worship him and not worship You, and the lām (اللام) is for the consequence (العاقبة). رَبَّنَا اطْمِسْ عَلى أَمْوالِهِمْ ** (Our Lord! Destroy their wealth) Destroy it (أهلكها) and obliterate it (امحقها). وَاشْدُدْ عَلى قُلُوبِهِمْ ** (and harden their hearts) And make them hard (أقْسِهَا) and seal them (واطبع عليها) so that they do not open up to faith (للإيمان). فَلا يُؤْمِنُوا حَتَّى يَرَوُا الْعَذابَ الْأَلِيمَ ** (so that they will not believe until they see the painful punishment.') Since he no longer had hope (طمع) in their faith (إيمانهم), his anger against them intensified, so he supplicated to Allah against them with what He knew would inevitably occur, so that he might bear witness against them that they deserve nothing but abandonment (الخذلان) and that He should leave them to their misguidance (اضلالهم). And the meaning of al-tams (الطّمس) upon the wealth (الأموال) is changing it from its state to a state by which it cannot be benefited. It was said: All their wealth turned into stones (حجارة).

(89) قالَ قَدْ أُجِيبَتْ دَعْوَتُكُما ** (He said: 'Your prayer has been answered.') Meaning Moses and Aaron. It was said that Moses was the supplicator (داعيًا) and Aaron was the one saying Amin (يؤمّن), so He named them both supplicators (داعين).

In al-Kafi, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: Moses supplicated, Aaron said Amin, and the angels said Amin. Allah the Almighty said: قَدْ أُجِيبَتْ دَعْوَتُكُما ** (Your prayer has been answered.) And whoever fights (غزا) in the way of Allah, his prayer is answered, just as it was answered for you both on the Day of Resurrection. فَاسْتَقِيما ** (So be steadfast) So be firm upon what you are upon regarding the call (الدعوة) and the establishment of the proof (الزام الحجّة), and do not hasten, for indeed, what you have requested will come to pass, but in its appointed time.

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): There were forty years between the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, قَدْ أُجِيبَتْ دَعْوَتُكُما (Your prayer has been answered), and the seizing of Pharaoh.

And in al-Khisal, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Allah granted Pharaoh respite (أملى) for forty years between the two words. Then Allah seized him as a deterrent for the Hereafter and the former life. And there were forty years between when Allah said to Moses and Aaron, قَدْ أُجِيبَتْ دَعْوَتُكُما (Your prayer has been answered), and when He informed them of the answer (الإجابة). Then he said: Gabriel said: "I contended with my Lord concerning Pharaoh with a severe contention (منازلة شديدة)." So I said: "O Lord, do You leave him alone when he has said: **أَنَا رَبُّكُمُ الْأَعْلى ** (I am your Lord, the most high)?" He said: "Only a servant like you says such a thing."

وَلا تَتَّبِعانِ ** (And do not follow) And it was recited with the takhfif (lightening) of the nūn. سَبِيلَ الَّذِينَ لا يَعْلَمُونَ (the way of those who do not know) The path of the ignorant (الجهلة) in hastening and lacking trust (الوثوق) and assurance (الاطمئنان) in the promise of Allah.

(٩٠) وَجاوَزْنا بِبَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ الْبَحْرَ ** We crossed with them until they passed over it safely فَأَتْبَعَهُمْ ** He pursued them/caught up with them **فِرْعَوْنُ وَجُنُودُهُ بَغْياً وَعَدْواً ** being oppressive and aggressive.

(٩٠) **وَجاوَزْنا بِبَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ الْبَحْرَ فَأَتْبَعَهُمْ فِرْعَوْنُ وَجُنُودُهُ بَغْياً وَعَدْواً حَتَّى إِذا أَدْرَكَهُ الْغَرَقُ قالَ آمَنْتُ أَنَّهُ لا إِلهَ إِلَّا الَّذِي آمَنَتْ بِهِ بَنُوا إِسْرائِيلَ وَأَنَا مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ ** (90) We carried the Children of Israel across the sea. Then Pharaoh and his hosts pursued them, out of tyranny and aggression, until, when the drowning overtook him, he said, 'I believe that there is no god except Him in Whom the Children of Israel believe, and I am one of the submitters.'

In al-Ayyashi, narrated marfu'an (مرفوعاً): When Moses entered the sea, Pharaoh and his hosts pursued him. He said: Pharaoh's horse hesitated to enter the sea. So Gabriel (جبرئيل) appeared to him upon a mare (رَمَكة). When Pharaoh's horse saw the mare (الرّمكّة), it followed her, and he and his companions entered the sea, and they drowned. حَتَّى إِذا أَدْرَكَهُ الْغَرَقُ قالَ آمَنْتُ أَنَّهُ And it has been recited with a kasrah (بالكسر) based on al-istīnaf (starting a new clause) (على الاستيناف) لا إِلهَ إِلَّا الَّذِي آمَنَتْ بِهِ بَنُوا إِسْرائِيلَ وَأَنَا مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ . He repeated the single meaning three times with three expressions, out of eagerness for acceptance. Yet, it was not accepted from him, as he missed its proper time and said it at the time of compulsion (الإلجاءِ). Whereas a single instance would have been sufficient at the time of choice (الإختيار) and the continuation of religious obligation (التكليف).

(٩١) آلْآنَ ** Do you believe now, when you have despaired of yourself and no choice remains for you? وَقَدْ عَصَيْتَ قَبْلُ ** before that, throughout the duration of your life, وَكُنْتَ مِنَ الْمُفْسِدِينَ ** the misguided (الضّالّين) who lead others astray (المضلّين) from faith (الإيمان).

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Gabriel (peace be upon him) never came to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم except that he was sorrowful (كئيباً) and grieved (حزيناً), and he remained so ever since Allah destroyed Pharaoh. But when Allah commanded him to descend with this verse: وَقَدْ عَصَيْتَ قَبْلُ وَكُنْتَ مِنَ الْمُفْسِدِينَ (while you disobeyed before, and you were one of the agents of corruption?), he descended upon him while laughing (ضاحك) and joyful (مستبشر).

So the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "O Gabriel, you never came to me except that grief was evident on your face, until this hour."

He said: "Yes, O Muhammad. When Allah drowned Pharaoh, **قالَ آمَنْتُ أَنَّهُ لا إِلهَ إِلَّا الَّذِي آمَنَتْ بِهِ بَنُوا إِسْرائِيلَ وَأَنَا مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ ** (90) He said, 'I believe that there is no god except Him in Whom the Children of Israel believe, and I am one of the submitters.')

So I took mud (حَمَأة) and placed it in his mouth (فيه), then I said to him: آلْآنَ وَقَدْ عَصَيْتَ قَبْلُ وَكُنْتَ مِنَ الْمُفْسِدِينَ (Now, when you disobeyed before and you were one of the agents of corruption?)

And I did that without the command of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ). Then I feared that mercy (الرّحمة) from Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, might reach him, and that Allah might punish me for what I had done. But when it was now, and Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, commanded me to convey to you what I had said to Pharaoh, I felt secure (آمنت) and knew that this was pleasing (رضاً) to Allah, the Exalted (تعالى)."

(٩٢) فَالْيَوْمَ نُنَجِّيكَ بِبَدَنِكَ ** (92) So today We will deliver you with your body We save you, stripped of the soul, from what your people fell into in the sea, or We cast you upon a high ground (نَجْوَة) of the earth—which is the elevated place—so that the Children of Israel may see you, لِتَكُونَ لِمَنْ خَلْفَكَ ** and are behind you, who are the Children of Israel, آيَةً ** that you may be a sign for those who succeed you (92) a sign (آيَةً), a mark (علامة) by which your servitude (عبوديّتك) and humiliation (مهانتك) become manifest to them, and that what you used to claim of Lordship (الربوبيّة) is impossible. For in their hearts, they believed that Pharaoh was too exalted in status to drown.

In al-Qummi, [it is narrated] that Moses informed the Children of Israel that Allah had drowned Pharaoh, but they did not believe him. So, Allah commanded the sea, and it cast him out onto the seashore until they saw him dead. The complete discussion concerning this will follow. وَإِنَّ كَثِيراً مِنَ النَّاسِ عَنْ آياتِنا لَغافِلُونَ ** (92) Indeed many of the people are oblivious to Our signs.** They do not reflect upon them nor take heed.

In al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): He was asked for what reason Allah the Almighty drowned Pharaoh, even though he had believed in Him and affirmed His Oneness (توحيده). He said: "Because he believed upon seeing the affliction (البأس), and faith upon seeing the affliction is not accepted. This is according to the judgment of Allah, exalted be His remembrance, concerning those who came before and those who came after." The Almighty Allah said: فَلَمَّا رَأَوْا بَأْسَنا قالُوا آمَنَّا بِاللهِ وَحْدَهُ وَكَفَرْنا بِما كُنَّا بِهِ مُشْرِكِينَ فَلَمْ يَكُ يَنْفَعُهُمْ إِيمانُهُمْ لَمَّا رَأَوْا بَأْسَنا (Then, when they saw Our might, they said, 'We believe in Allah alone, and we disbelieve in what we used to associate with Him.' But their faith was of no avail to them when they saw Our might.) And He, the Mighty and Majestic, said: يَوْمَ يَأْتِي بَعْضُ آياتِ رَبِّكَ لا يَنْفَعُ نَفْساً إِيمانُها لَمْ تَكُنْ آمَنَتْ مِنْ قَبْلُ أَوْ كَسَبَتْ فِي إِيمانِها خَيْراً ** (158) The day when some of the signs of your Lord come, a soul's faith will not benefit it, if it had not believed before or earned some good in its faith. And likewise, Pharaoh, when أَدْرَكَهُ الْغَرَقُ قالَ آمَنْتُ أَنَّهُ لا إِلهَ إِلَّا الَّذِي آمَنَتْ بِهِ بَنُوا إِسْرائِيلَ وَأَنَا مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ ** (90) drowning overtook him, said, 'I believe that there is no god except Him in whom the Children of Israel believe, and I am one of the submitters.' So it was said to him: آلْآنَ وَقَدْ عَصَيْتَ قَبْلُ وَكُنْتَ مِنَ الْمُفْسِدِينَ فَالْيَوْمَ نُنَجِّيكَ بِبَدَنِكَ لِتَكُونَ لِمَنْ خَلْفَكَ ** (91) 'Now? While you disobeyed before and were one of the agents of corruption! (92) So today We will deliver you with your body that you may be a sign for those who succeed you.' a sign (آية). And Pharaoh was covered in iron from his horn (قَرْن) to his foot (قَدَم); he had worn it upon his body. When he drowned, Allah the Almighty cast him upon a high ground (نَجْوَة) of the earth with his body, so that He might be a sign (علامة) for those who came after him. Thus, they would see him, despite his heaviness due to the iron, upon an elevated place of the earth, whereas the way of the heavy object is to sink and not rise. This was therefore a sign (آية) and a mark (علامة). And for another reason did Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, drown him: namely, that he sought help from Moses when drowning overtook him, and he did not seek help from Allah the Almighty. So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed to him: "O Moses, why did you not help Pharaoh? Because you did not create him. Had he sought help from Me, I would have helped Him."

And in al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding this verse: The Children of Israel said: "O Moses, supplicate to Allah to grant us relief (فَرَجاً) from what we are in." So he supplicated, and Allah revealed to Him: "Travel with them." He said: "O Lord, the sea is before them." He said: "Proceed, for I command it to obey you and part for you." So Moses departed with the Children of Israel, and Pharaoh followed them until he was about to overtake them, and Moses saw that they had been overshadowed by him. Moses said to the sea: "Part for me." It said: "I would not do that." And the Children of Israel said to Moses: "You deceived us and destroyed us! Would that you had left us so that Pharaoh's people could enslave us, and we had not come out! Now we will be killed a terrible death." قالَ كَلَّا إِنَّ مَعِي رَبِّي سَيَهْدِينِ ** (62) He said, 'Certainly not! My Lord is with me; He will guide me.' What the generality of his people were doing to him became severe for Moses, and they said: "O Moses, إِنَّا لَمُدْرَكُونَ ** (61) We are surely overtaken!" "You claimed that the sea would part for us so that we could pass and go, yet Pharaoh and his people have overwhelmed us, and here they are—you see them approaching us." So Moses supplicated to his Lord, and Allah revealed to Him: أَنِ اضْرِبْ بِعَصاكَ الْبَحْرَ ** (63) 'Strike the sea with your staff.' So he struck it, and the sea parted. Moses and his companions proceeded until they crossed the sea. Pharaoh's people overtook them. When they looked at the sea, they said to Pharaoh: "Are you not amazed by what you see?" He said: "I did this." So they passed and proceeded into it. When Pharaoh and those with him reached the middle, Allah commanded the sea, and it closed upon them, drowning them all. When drowning overtook Pharaoh, قالَ آمَنْتُ أَنَّهُ لا إِلهَ إِلَّا الَّذِي آمَنَتْ بِهِ بَنُوا إِسْرائِيلَ وَأَنَا مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ ** (90) he said, 'I believe that there is no god except Him in whom the Children of Israel believe, and I am one of the submitters.' Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, says: آلْآنَ وَقَدْ عَصَيْتَ قَبْلُ وَكُنْتَ مِنَ الْمُفْسِدِينَ ** (91) 'Now? While you disobeyed before and were one of the agents of corruption!' He says: "You were one of the disobedient." فَالْيَوْمَ نُنَجِّيكَ بِبَدَنِكَ He said: Indeed, Pharaoh's people all went into the sea, and none of them were seen; they plunged into the sea toward the Fire. As for Pharaoh, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, cast him out (نَبَذَهُ) and threw him onto the seashore so that they might look at him and recognize him, لِتَكُونَ لِمَنْ خَلْفَكَ ** a sign (آية). And so that no one would doubt his destruction, for they had taken him as a lord (رَبّاً). So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, showed them a corpse (جِيفَةً) cast upon the seashore, so that he might be a lesson (عِبْرَةً) and an admonition (عِظَةً) for those who came after him. Allah says: وَإِنَّ كَثِيراً مِنَ النَّاسِ عَنْ آياتِنا لَغافِلُونَ. (92) Indeed many of the people are oblivious to Our signs.

(٩٣) وَلَقَدْ بَوَّأْنا بَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ مُبَوَّأَ صِدْقٍ ** (93) We certainly settled the Children of Israel in a worthy settlement (meaning) a righteous, pleasing dwelling, which is Syria (الشّام) and Egypt (مصر). Al-Qummi (said that He) returned them to Egypt (مصر) and Pharaoh (فرعون) drowned. وَرَزَقْناهُمْ مِنَ الطَّيِّباتِ ** and provided them with good things (meaning) from the delights (اللذائذ). فَمَا اخْتَلَفُوا ** Then they did not differ regarding the matter of their religion, nor did they split into factions (شُعَباً), حَتَّى جاءَهُمُ الْعِلْمُ ** until knowledge came to them (meaning) with the true religion (دين الحقّ), and they read the Torah (التورية) and knew its rulings (أحكامها); or (the difference was) regarding the matter of Muhammad صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم, except after they knew his truthfulness through his descriptions (نعوته) and the abundance of his miracles (معجزاته). إِنَّ رَبَّكَ يَقْضِي بَيْنَهُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ فِيما كانُوا فِيهِ يَخْتَلِفُونَ ** Your Lord will indeed judge between them on the Day of Resurrection concerning that about which they used to differ. Thus, He will distinguish the truthful (المحقّ) from the false (المبطِل) through salvation (الإنجاءِ) and destruction (الإهلاك).

(٩٤) فَإِنْ كُنْتَ (١) فِي شَكٍّ مِمَّا أَنْزَلْنا إِلَيْكَ فَسْئَلِ الَّذِينَ يَقْرَؤُنَ الْكِتابَ مِنْ قَبْلِكَ لَقَدْ جاءَكَ الْحَقُّ مِنْ رَبِّكَ فَلا تَكُونَنَّ مِنَ المُمْتَرِينَ. (94) If you are in doubt concerning what We have sent down to you, then ask those who read the Book before you. The truth has certainly come to you from your Lord, so do not be among the skeptics.

footnote (١) It was said that the meaning is: If doubt were to occur to you, hypothetically and presumptively, then ask the scholars of the People of the Book (*أهل الكتاب*), for they encompass knowledge regarding the correctness of what was sent down to you.

(٩٥) وَلا تَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا بِآياتِ اللهِ فَتَكُونَ مِنَ الْخاسِرِينَ. (95) And do not be one of those who deny the signs of Allah, lest you should be among the losers.

In al-Ilal and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Hadi (peace be upon him): His brother Musa asked him about this verse when Yahya ibn Aktham wrote to him asking him about issues, among which was: Inform me who is addressed by the verse? If the one addressed is the Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم, then he did not doubt what Allah revealed. And if the one addressed is other than him, then the Book was revealed to someone other than him. Musa said: So I asked my brother Ali ibn Muhammad (peace be upon them) about that, and he said: The one addressed by that is the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and He was not in doubt about what Allah revealed. However, the ignorant ones (الجهلة) said: Why does He not send us a prophet from the angels to distinguish between him and others regarding independence from food and drink in the markets? So Allah revealed to His Prophet, فَسْئَلِ الَّذِينَ يَقْرَؤُنَ الْكِتابَ مِنْ قَبْلِكَ ** (So ask those who read the Book before you) in the presence of the ignorant ones (الجهلة): Did Allah send any messenger before you except that he ate food and walked in the markets? And you have an example in them. He only said فَإِنْ كُنْتَ فِي شَكٍ ** (If you are in doubt), and He was not, but rather so that they might follow them, just as He said: فَقُلْ تَعالَوْا نَدْعُ أَبْناءَنا وَأَبْناءَكُمْ وَنِساءَنا وَنِساءَكُمْ وَأَنْفُسَنا وَأَنْفُسَكُمْ ثُمَّ نَبْتَهِلْ فَنَجْعَلْ لَعْنَتَ اللهِ عَلَى الْكاذِبِينَ (Say, 'Come, let us call our sons and your sons, our women and your women, and ourselves and yourselves. Then let us pray, and invoke Allah’s curse on the liars'). If He had said, 'Come, let us pray and invoke Allah’s curse upon you,' they would not have responded to the Mubahalah (المباهلة). He already knew that His Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم was conveying His message on His behalf and was not among the liars. Likewise, the Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم knew that He was truthful in what He said, but He loved to be fair (يُنْصِف) from Himself.

And in al-Ilal, the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "I do not doubt, nor do I ask."

And al-Qummi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): When the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم was taken on the Night Journey (أُسْرِيَ) to the heaven, and Allah revealed to Him concerning Ali (peace be upon him) what He revealed regarding his honor and his greatness before Allah, and He was returned to al-Bayt al-Ma'mur (البيت المعمُور) and the Prophets were gathered for Him and they prayed behind Him, a thought occurred in the soul of the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم due to the greatness of what was revealed to Him concerning Ali (peace be upon him). So Allah revealed: فَإِنْ كُنْتَ فِي شَكٍّ مِمَّا أَنْزَلْنا إِلَيْكَ فَسْئَلِ الَّذِينَ يَقْرَؤُنَ الْكِتابَ مِنْ قَبْلِكَ (If you are in doubt concerning what We have sent down to you, then ask those who read the Book before you)—meaning the Prophets—"for We have indeed sent down to them in their books concerning his merit (فضله) what We have sent down to you in your Book." لَقَدْ جاءَكَ الْحَقُّ مِنْ رَبِّكَ فَلا تَكُونَنَّ مِنَ المُمْتَرِينَ (The truth has certainly come to you from your Lord, so do not be among the skeptics) وَلا تَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا بِآياتِ اللهِ فَتَكُونَ مِنَ الْخاسِرِينَ (And do not be one of those who deny the signs of Allah, lest you should be among the losers). Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) then said: "By Allah, He neither doubted nor asked."

And al-Ayyashi narrated something close to it, and there are other narrations (أخبار أخر) with the same meaning. A similar discussion will come in Surat al-Zukhruf, if Allah wills. And according to both narrations, the address (الخطاب) is of the type: "I mean you, but listen, O neighbor (إيّاك أعني واسمعي يا جارة)."

footnote And it was said: Rather, the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله was addressed, but the intended meaning is his community (*أمّته*), and the meaning is: "If you (plural) are in doubt." And it was said: The address is directed to the listener for whom doubt is permissible, like their saying: "If your brother is mighty, then be humble (*إذا عزّ أخوك فهُن*)." And the inconsistency (*التّهافت*) in these sayings is not hidden, for how can the People of the Book (*أهل الكتاب*) confirm him while he is in doubt about his matter? And if they do not confirm him, then they would be inviting him to their religion. Furthermore, what was revealed of the revelation (*الوحي*) was only revealed to him and was not revealed to the community (*الأمّة*), so how can the community be addressed by it? [From him, may Allah have mercy on him.]

(٩٦) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ حَقَّتْ عَلَيْهِمْ ** has been confirmed/established ** كَلِمَتُ رَبِّكَ ** that they will die upon disbelief (الكفر) ** لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ ** since His word is not belied, nor is His decree diminished. (96) Indeed those against whom the word of your Lord has been realized will not believe. (٩٧) ** وَلَوْ جَاءَتْهُمْ كُلُّ آيَةٍ حَتَّى يَرَوُا الْعَذَابَ الْأَلِيمَ ** and at that point, it will not benefit them, just as it did not benefit Pharaoh (فرعون). (97) Even if every sign were to come to them, until they see the painful punishment. Al-Qummi (القمّيّ): Those who denied the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him)—the guardianship (الولاية) was presented to them, and Allah obligated them to believe in it, but they did not believe in it.

(98) فَلَوْ لا كانَتْ قَرْيَةٌ آمَنَتْ ** Why was there not كانَتْ قَرْيَةٌ ** (a town) among the towns We destroyed آمَنَتْ ** (that believed) before witnessing the punishment, and did not delay it until it reached them, just as Pharaoh delayed until the drowning overtook him, so that its faith would benefit it, by Allah accepting it from them and removing the punishment from them, إِلَّا قَوْمَ يُونُسَ ** (except the people of Yunus)? But the people of Yunus لَمَّا آمَنُوا ** (when they believed) the moment they saw the sign (إمارة) of the punishment, and did not delay it until its descent, كَشَفْنا عَنْهُمْ عَذابَ الْخِزْيِ فِي الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا وَمَتَّعْناهُمْ إِلى حِينٍ ** (We removed from them the punishment of disgrace in the life of the world, and We provided for them for a while). It is permissible for the sentence to carry the meaning of negation, because the particle of specification (حرف التخصيص) implies that meaning, making the exception (الاستثناء) continuous (متصلاً). It is as if it was said: No town among the perished towns believed إِلَّا قَوْمَ يُونُسَ. (except the people of Yunus).

In al-Jawami': Yunus (peace be upon him) had been sent to Nineveh (نينوى) (1) in the land of Mosul (الموصل). They rejected him, so he departed from them in anger (مغاضباً). When they missed him, they feared the descent of the punishment. So they wore sackcloth (المسوح), cried out (عجّوا), and wept. Thus, Allah averted the punishment from them, even though it had already descended and was close to them.

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from Abu Ubaydah al-Hadhdha', from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him), who said: The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) wrote, saying: The Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his progeny and grant them peace) narrated to me that Gabriel narrated to him that Yunus son of Matta (peace be upon him) was sent by Allah to his people when he was thirty years old. He was a man prone to sharpness (الحدّة) and was impatient with his people and lacked forbearance (المداراة) towards them, being incapable of bearing the heavy burden (ثقل حمل أوقار) (1) of Prophethood and its signs (أعلامها), and that he would collapse under it just as a tree trunk collapses under its load. He remained among them, calling them to faith in Allah, belief in Him, and following Him for thirty-three years. Yet, none of his people believed in him or followed him except for two men. The name of one was Rubil, and the name of the other was Tanukha. Rubil was from the household of knowledge, prophethood, and wisdom, and had been a long-time companion of Yunus son of Matta (peace be upon him) before Allah sent him with Prophethood. Tanukha was a weak, devout, ascetic man, immersed in worship, possessing neither knowledge nor wisdom. Rubil was a shepherd who tended sheep and earned his sustenance from them. Tanukha was a woodcutter who carried wood on his head and ate from his earnings. Rubil held a status with Yunus different from Tanukha's, due to Rubil's knowledge, wisdom, and long companionship. When Yunus saw that his people would not respond to him or believe, he became distressed and recognized his own lack of patience. He complained of this to his Lord. Among his complaints, he said: "O Lord, You sent me to my people when I was thirty years old, and I remained among them, calling them to faith in You, believing in my message, and warning them of Your punishment and vengeance for thirty-three years. But they rejected me, did not believe in me, denied my Prophethood, and belittled my message. They have threatened me, and I fear they will kill me. So, send down Your punishment upon them, for they are a people who will not believe." He (Imam al-Baqir) said: So Allah revealed to Yunus: "Indeed, among them are the pregnant woman, the fetus, the child, the old man, the elderly, the weak woman, and the despised weakling. I am the Just Judge. My Mercy precedes My Wrath. I do not punish the young for the sins of the elders among your people. O Yunus, they are My servants, My creation, and My creatures in My lands and under My care (عيلتي). I love to treat them gently (أتاناهم) and be kind to them, and I await their repentance. I only sent you to your people to be a guardian (حفيظاً) over them, to show them compassion with the overflowing measures (سجال) of Mercy that touch them, to treat them gently with the tenderness of Prophethood, to endure with them through the forbearance (أحلام) of the Message, and to be to them like a healing physician who knows the remedy of the medicine. But you departed from them without treating their hearts with kindness (الرّفق) and without governing them with the policy (سياسة) of the Messengers. Then you asked Me for punishment for them due to your poor judgment (سوء نظرك) and your lack of patience. My servant Noah was more patient than you with his people, had better companionship, was more enduring in patience with Me, and more eloquent in offering excuses. So, I became angry for him when he became angry for Me, and I answered him when he called upon Me." Yunus said: "O Lord, I only became angry with them for Your sake, and I only called down punishment upon them when they disobeyed You. By Your Might, I will never show them compassion (أتعطف) again, nor will I look upon them with the advice of a compassionate one (نصيحة شفيق) after their disbelief, their rejection of me, and their denial of my Prophethood. So, send down Your punishment upon them, for they will never believe." So Allah Almighty said: "O Yunus, they are one hundred thousand or more of My creation. They inhabit My lands and give birth to My servants. My love is that I treat them gently, due to what preceded in My knowledge concerning them and concerning you. My decree (تقديري) and My management (تدبيري) are different from your knowledge and your decree. You are the Messenger, and I am the Wise Lord. My knowledge concerning them, O Yunus, is hidden in the Unseen with Me; its ultimate end (منتهاه) is unknown. Your knowledge concerning them is apparent, with no hidden aspect. O Yunus, I have answered your request to send down the punishment upon them. But that, O Yunus, is neither the most abundant share (أوفر لحظك) for you from Me nor the most praiseworthy for your status (شأنك). A punishment will come to them in Shawwal, on a Wednesday, in the middle of the month, after sunrise. Inform them of this." He said: Yunus was pleased by this and was not distressed, but he did not know its consequence (عاقبته). So Yunus went to Tanukha the worshipper and informed him of what Allah had revealed to him regarding the descent of the punishment upon his people on that day. He said to him: "Let us go and inform them of what Allah has revealed to me concerning the descent of the punishment." Tanukha said: "Leave them in their heedlessness (غمرتهم) and disobedience until Allah punishes them." Yunus said to him: "No, rather let us meet Rubil and consult him, for he is a knowledgeable, wise man from the household of Prophethood." So they went to Rubil. Yunus informed him of what Allah had revealed to him regarding the descent of the punishment upon his people in Shawwal, on a Wednesday, in the middle of the month, after sunrise. He said to him: "What is your opinion? Let us go and inform them of this." Rubil said to him: "Return to your Lord with the return of a wise Prophet and a noble Messenger, and ask Him to avert the punishment from them, for He is independent of punishing them, and He loves kindness (الرفق) towards His servants. That is neither a burden (إصر) (1) for you with Him nor a detriment (أسرى) to your status with Him. Perhaps your people, after what you have heard and seen of their disbelief and denial, will believe one day. So be patient with them and treat them gently." Tanukha said to him: "Woe to you, O Rubil! How can you advise Yunus and command him to do this after their disbelief in Allah, their denial of His Prophet, their rejection of him, their expulsion of him from their dwellings, and their intention to stone him?" Rubil said to Tanukha: "Be silent, for you are a worshipper who has no knowledge." Then he turned to Yunus and said: "Tell me, O Yunus, when Allah sends down the punishment upon your people, will He send it down to destroy them all, or will He destroy some and spare others?" Yunus said to him: "Rather, He will destroy them all. That is what I asked Him for, as no compassionate mercy entered my heart for them, so that I might return to Allah concerning them and ask Him to avert it from them." Rubil said to him: "Do you know, O Yunus, perhaps when Allah sends down the punishment upon them and they sense it, they will repent to Him and seek forgiveness, and He will have mercy on them, for He is the Most Merciful of the merciful? He will remove the punishment from them after you informed them on behalf of Allah Almighty that the punishment would descend upon them on Wednesday, and you would thereby become a liar in their eyes." Tanukha said to him: "Woe to you, O Rubil! You have said a terrible thing! The sent Prophet informs you that Allah revealed to him that the punishment will descend upon them, yet you reject the saying of Allah Almighty and doubt Him and the saying of His Messenger! Go, for your deeds have been nullified (حبط عملك)." Rubil said to Tanukha: "Your opinion is corrupted." Then he turned to Yunus and said: "The revelation and the command from Allah concerning them have descended upon you regarding the sending down of the punishment upon them, and His word is the truth. Tell me, if that happens, and all your people perish and their town is ruined, will Allah not erase your name from Prophethood, invalidate your Message, and make you like some of the weak people, while a hundred thousand people perish at your hands?" But Yunus refused to accept his advice. So he departed, accompanied by Tanukha, to his people and informed them that Allah had revealed to him that He would send down the punishment upon them on Wednesday in Shawwal, in the middle of the month, after sunrise. They rejected his word, called him a liar, and violently expelled him from their town. Yunus and Tanukha left the town and withdrew from them not far away, remaining there awaiting the punishment. Rubil stayed with his people in their town. When Shawwal began, Rubil shouted at the top of his voice from the mountaintop to the people: "I am Rubil, the compassionate towards you, the merciful towards you before his Lord! You have denied the punishment of Allah! This is Shawwal that has begun for you, and Yunus, your Prophet and the Messenger of your Lord, informed you that Allah revealed to him that the punishment will descend upon you in Shawwal, in the middle of the month, on Wednesday, after sunrise. Allah will not break His promise to His Messengers. So, look at what you are going to do!" His words terrified them, and the certainty of the punishment's descent fell into their hearts. They rushed towards Rubil and said to him: "What do you advise us to do, O Rubil? You are a knowledgeable, wise man. We have always known you for your tenderness towards us and your mercy towards us. We have been informed of what you advised Yunus. So, command us with your order and advise us with your opinion." Rubil said to them: "I see for you and advise you that you should wait and intend, when the dawn breaks on Wednesday in the middle of the month, to separate the children from their mothers at the bottom of the mountain, in the path of the valleys, and station the women on the slope of the mountain. All of this must be before sunrise. Then cry out, the great among you and the small, with screaming, weeping, supplication to Allah, repentance to Him, and seeking His forgiveness. Raise your heads to the sky and say: 'Our Lord, we have wronged ourselves and denied Your Prophet. We have repented to You from our sins. If You do not forgive us and have mercy on us, we shall surely be among the losers, the punished ones. Accept our repentance and have mercy on us, O Most Merciful of the merciful!' Then do not tire of weeping, screaming, supplication to Allah, and repentance to Him until the sun is concealed by the veil, or until Allah removes the punishment from you before that." The opinion of all the people unanimously agreed to do what Rubil had advised them. When Wednesday arrived, the day they expected the punishment, Rubil withdrew from the town to a place where he could hear their screams and see the punishment if it descended. When the dawn broke on Wednesday, the people of Yunus did what Rubil had commanded them. When the sun rose, a dark, swift yellow wind approached, making a whistling (صرير) and rustling (حفيف) sound (1). When they saw it, they all cried out with screaming, weeping, and supplication to Allah. They repented and sought His forgiveness. The children screamed, seeking their mothers. The young of the livestock cried out, seeking the udder. The cattle rushed about, seeking pasture. They continued in this state, while Yunus and Tanukha heard their cries and screams, and they prayed to Allah to intensify the punishment upon them. Rubil, in his place, heard their screams and cries and saw what had descended, and he prayed to Allah to remove the punishment from them. When the sun passed its zenith (زالت الشمس), the gates of heaven were opened, and the wrath of the Lord Almighty subsided, the All-merciful had mercy on them. He answered their prayer, accepted their repentance, and overlooked their slip (أقالهم عثرتهم). He revealed to Israfil: "Descend to the people of Yunus, for they have cried out with weeping and supplication, and they have repented to Me and sought My forgiveness. So, I have had mercy on them and accepted their repentance. I am Allah, the Oft-Forgiving (التّوابُ), the All-merciful (الرّحيم). I hasten to accept the repentance of My servant who repents from sin. My servant Yunus, My Messenger, had asked Me for the descent of the punishment upon his people, and I have sent it down upon them. I am Allah, the most worthy of fulfilling His covenant, and I have sent it down upon them. However, Yunus did not stipulate, when he asked Me to send down the punishment upon them, that I should destroy them. So, descend to them and avert from them what has descended upon them of My punishment." Israfil said: "O Lord, Your punishment has reached their sides (أكنافهم) (1) and is about to destroy them. I see it as having already descended upon their courtyard (ساحتهم). Where should I avert it?" Allah said: "No! I have commanded My angels to avert it and not to send it down upon them until My command and My decree (عزيمتي) concerning them reach them. So, descend, O Israfil, upon them and avert it from them. Avert it towards the mountains, the area where the springs flow (مفاض العيون), and the courses of the torrents (مجاري السّيُول) in the mighty, aggressive mountains that tower over the mountains. Humble them with it and soften them until they become solid, hard iron." So Israfil descended and spread his wings, driving that punishment with them until he struck those mountains to which Allah had revealed that he should avert it. Abu Ja'far (peace be upon him) said: "These are the mountains in the region of Mosul (الموصل) today, and they became iron until the Day of Resurrection." When the people of Yunus saw that the punishment had been averted from them, they descended to their dwellings from the mountaintops, gathered their women, children, and wealth, and praised Allah for what He had averted from them. Yunus and Tanukha awoke on Thursday in the place where they had been, having no doubt that the punishment had descended upon them and destroyed them all, since their voices had ceased to reach them. They approached the town on Thursday with the sunrise, looking to see what had become of the people. When they drew near to the people, they were met by the woodcutters (الحطّابون), the donkey-drivers (الحمّارة) (2), and the shepherds (الرّعاة) with their necks raised, and they saw the people of the town secure. Yunus said to Tanukha: "O Tanukha, the revelation has proven me a liar, and I have lied about My promise to My people. No, by the Might of my Lord, they will never see my face again after the revelation has proven me a liar." So Yunus departed, fleeing aimlessly, angry with his Lord (مغاضباً لربّه), towards the sea of Ayla (أيلة) (3), disguised (مُتنكراً), fleeing lest any of his people see him and say to him: "O liar!" That is why Allah said: وَذَا النُّونِ إِذْ ذَهَبَ مُغاضِباً فَظَنَّ أَنْ لَنْ نَقْدِرَ عَلَيْهِ ** (And [mention] Dhul-Nun, when he went away in anger, and thought that We would not put him in distress) the Ayah. Tanukha returned to the town and met Rubil. Rubil said to him: "O Tanukha, which of the two opinions was more correct and rightful? My opinion or yours?" Tanukha said to him: "Rather, your opinion was more correct, and you advised with the opinion of the scholars and the wise." Tanukha also said to him: "Indeed, I always thought that I was better than you because of my asceticism (زهدي) and the superiority of my worship, until your superiority became clear due to the superiority of your knowledge and what your Lord granted you of wisdom along with piety (التقوى), which is better than asceticism and worship without knowledge." So they remained companions, residing with their people. Yunus went on his way, angry with his Lord, and his story unfolded as Allah informed about it in His Book. So they believed, فَمَتَّعْناهُمْ إِلى حِينٍ (and We provided for them for a while). Abu Ubaydah said: I asked Abu Ja'far (peace be upon him): "How long was Yunus absent from his people until he returned to them with Prophethood and the Message, and they believed in him and affirmed him?" He said: "Four weeks: seven [days] in his journey to the sea, seven in the belly of the whale, seven under the tree in the open (بالعراءِ), and seven in his return to his people." I asked him: "Are these weeks months, days, or hours?" He said: "O Abu Ubaydah, the punishment came to them on Wednesday in the middle of Shawwal, and it was averted from them on that very day. Yunus departed in anger, so Thursday passed, then seven days in his journey to the sea, seven days in the belly of the whale, seven days under the tree in the open, and seven days in his return to his people. His departure and return totaled twenty-eight days. Then he came to them, and they believed in him, affirmed him, and followed him. That is why Allah said: فَلَوْ لا كانَتْ قَرْيَةٌ آمَنَتْ فَنَفَعَها إِيمانُها إِلَّا قَوْمَ يُونُسَ لَمَّا آمَنُوا كَشَفْنا عَنْهُمْ عَذابَ الْخِزْيِ فِي الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا وَمَتَّعْناهُمْ إِلى حِينٍ.** (Why was there no town that believed, so that its faith might benefit it, except the people of Yunus? When they believed, We removed from them the punishment of disgrace in the life of the world, and We provided for them for a while.)"

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): When his people harmed Yunus, he prayed to Allah against them. On the first day, they awoke with yellow faces, and on the second day, they awoke with black faces. He said: Allah had promised them that the punishment would come to them until they could reach it with their spears. So they separated the women from their children and the cows from their calves. They wore sackcloth (المسُوح) and wool, placed ropes around their necks, and ashes on their heads. They cried out with a single cry to their Lord and said: "We believe in the God of Yunus!" So Allah averted the punishment from them. Yunus awoke, thinking they had perished, but found them in well-being (عافية).

And in al-Ilal, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked for what reason Allah averted the punishment from the people of Yunus, even though it had overshadowed them, while He did not do the same for other nations? He said: "Because it was in Allah's knowledge that He would avert it from them due to their repentance. He only refrained from informing Yunus of this because He, the Mighty and Majestic, desired to free him for His worship in the belly of the whale, thereby making him deserving of His reward and honor."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Gabriel made an exception (استثنى) regarding the destruction of the people of Yunus, but Yunus did not hear it.

And al-Qummi agreed with al-Ayyashi in mentioning the story, except that he abbreviated it and mentioned the name of the worshipper as Malikha (مليخا) instead of Tanukha (تنوخا). He included other matters at the end of it, which We shall mention in Surat al-Saffat, if Allah wills, and some of his story will also come in Surat al-Anbiya, if Allah wills.

footnote (١) Nineveh (*نينوى*) with the first letter (*ن*) having a *kasra* (*كسرة*) is a place in Kufa (*الكوفة*) and a village in Mosul (*الموصل*) belonging to Yunus (peace be upon him).

footnote (١) The *wiqr* (*الوقر*) with a *kasra* (*كسرة*) is the heavy load, or more generally, a load. Its plural is *awqar* (*أوقار*).

footnote (١) *Al-Isr* (*الإصر*) is sin (*الذنب*) and heaviness (*الثّقل*). *Al-Isr* (*الإصر*) is also breaking. It is said: *Asartu al-amra asran* (*اصرت الأمر اصراً*) meaning: I broke it.

footnote (١) The horse's *haff* (*حفّ*) *hafīfan* (*حفيفاً*) is the sound heard when it runs. The viper's *fahh* (*فحّ*) *fahīhan* (*فحيحاً*) is the sound from its mouth, while the *hafīf* (*حفيف*) is from its skin. Likewise for the bird and the trees when they make a sound.

footnote (١) *Fi kanaf Allah* (*في كنف الله*) (with *fatha* on the *kāf*) means in His protection (*حرزه*) and cover (*ستره*). It is the side (*الجانب*), the shade (*الظل*), and the region (*الناحية*), like *al-kanafa* (*الكنفة*) (with *fatha* on the *kāf*).

footnote (٢) *Al-Hammara* (*الحمّارة*) like *jabbana* (*جبّانة*) means the owners of donkeys, like *al-hamira* (*الحامرة*).

footnote (٣) Ayla (*أيلة*) is a mountain between Mecca (*مكّة*) and Medina (*المدينة*) near Yanbu' (*ينبع*), and a town between Yanbu' (*ينبع*) and Egypt (*مصر*).

(٩٩) وَلَوْ شاءَ رَبُّكَ لَآمَنَ مَنْ فِي الْأَرْضِ كُلُّهُمْ ** (99) And had your Lord willed, everyone on earth would have believed so that none of them is excluded, all of them united upon faith, not differing concerning it. أَفَأَنْتَ تُكْرِهُ النَّاسَ حَتَّى يَكُونُوا مُؤْمِنِينَ.** (Will you then compel people until they become faithful?)

(100) وَما كانَ لِنَفْسٍ أَنْ تُؤْمِنَ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِ اللهِ وَيَجْعَلُ الرِّجْسَ (100) And it is not for any soul to believe except by Allah’s leave, and He lays the defilement

And it was read with the nūn (وَنَجْعَلُ) عَلَى الَّذِينَ لا يَعْقِلُونَ upon those who do not apply reason.

In al-Uyun, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): Al-Ma’mun asked him about this Ayah, so he said: "My father narrated to me, from his forefathers, from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon them), who said: 'The Muslims said to the Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his progeny): O Messenger of Allah, if you were to compel those among the people whom you have power over to embrace Islam, our numbers would increase, and we would be strong against our enemy.' The Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his progeny) said: 'I would not meet Allah with an innovation (بدعة) concerning which He has not revealed anything to me, nor am I one of those who impose burdens (المتكلّفين).' So Allah revealed to him: O Muhammad,

وَلَوْ شاءَ رَبُّكَ لَآمَنَ مَنْ فِي الْأَرْضِ كُلُّهُمْ جَمِيعاً (99) And had your Lord willed, everyone on earth would have believed, all of them together.

(This belief would be) by way of compulsion (الإلجاء) and necessity (الاضطرار) in this world, just as they will believe upon witnessing and seeing the affliction (البأس) in the Hereafter. And had I done that to them, they would not have deserved any reward (ثواباً) or praise (مدحاً) from Me. Rather, I desire them to believe by choice, not under compulsion, so that they may deserve closeness (الزّلفى), honor (الكرامة), and eternal permanence (دوام الخلُود) in the Garden of Eternity (جنّة الخلد) from Me.

أَفَأَنْتَ تُكْرِهُ النَّاسَ حَتَّى يَكُونُوا مُؤْمِنِينَ (99) Will you then compel the people until they become believers?

As for His saying: وَما كانَ لِنَفْسٍ أَنْ تُؤْمِنَ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِ اللهِ (100) And it is not for any soul to believe except by Allah’s leave,

this is not by way of prohibiting belief upon it, but rather it means that it would not believe except by Allah’s leave (إذن). And His leave (إذن) is His command to it to believe while it is still subject to responsibility (متكلّفة) and worship (متعبّدة), and His compelling (إلجاؤه) it to believe when the responsibility (التكليف) and worship (التعبّد) cease for it."

So Al-Ma’mun said: "You have relieved me; may Allah relieve you."

(١٠١) قُلِ انْظُرُوا ما ذا فِي السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ** (101) Say, 'Observe what is in the heavens and the earth.' (from) the wonders of His creation, so that He may guide you to His Oneness and the perfection of His power. وَما تُغْنِي الْآياتُ وَالنُّذُرُ عَنْ قَوْمٍ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ ** (and what avail the signs and the warners for a people who do not have faith?) whose faith is not expected. And ما is negating, or interrogative for denial (الإنكار).

In al-Kafi and al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: He was asked about this Ayah, and He said: "The signs (الْآياتُ) are the Imams عليهم السلام, and the warners (وَالنُّذُرُ) are the Prophets سلام الله عليهم."

(١٠٢) فَهَلْ يَنْتَظِرُونَ إِلَّا مِثْلَ أَيَّامِ الَّذِينَ خَلَوْا مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ ** (Do they await anything but the like of the days of those who passed away before them?) (It means) the like of their occurrences (وقائعهم) and the descent of Allah's might (بأس الله) upon them, since they deserve nothing else. قُلْ فَانْتَظِرُوا إِنِّي مَعَكُمْ مِنَ الْمُنْتَظِرِينَ ** (Say, 'Then wait! I too am waiting along with you.') for that (outcome).

Al-Ayyashi (العيّاشيّ) narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): Indeed, waiting for relief (انتظار الفَرَج) is part of the relief (الفَرَج), for Allah says: فَانْتَظِرُوا إِنِّي مَعَكُمْ مِنَ الْمُنْتَظِرِينَ (Then wait! I too am waiting along with you).

(١٠٣) ثُمَّ نُنَجِّي And it was read (وقرئ) with takhfif (lightening) رُسُلَنا وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا It is conjoined (عطف) to an omitted element (محذوف) indicated by what preceded it, as if it were said: We destroy the nations ثُمَّ نُنَجِّي رُسُلَنا and those who believed with them. كَذلِكَ حَقًّا عَلَيْنا نُنْجِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ And it was read (وقرئ) with tashdid (doubling), meaning: like that deliverance (الإنجاءِ), نُنْجِي الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ** (We deliver the faithful) from among you when We destroy the polytheists (المشركين). And حَقًّا عَلَيْنا ** is an interjection (اعتراض), meaning: the rightness (حقّ) of that is binding on Us, truly (حقّاً).

In al-Majma' and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): What prevents you from bearing witness concerning whoever among you dies upon this matter (هذا الأمرِ) that he is one of the people of Paradise (أهل الجنّة)? Indeed, Allah the Almighty says: كَذلِكَ حَقًّا عَلَيْنا نُنْجِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ. (Thus it is binding on Us to deliver the faithful.)

(١٠٤) قُلْ يا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ فِي شَكٍّ مِنْ دِينِي ** وصحّته فَلا أَعْبُدُ الَّذِينَ تَعْبُدُونَ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ وَلكِنْ أَعْبُدُ اللهَ الَّذِي يَتَوَفَّاكُمْ ** (104) Say, 'O mankind! If you are in doubt about my religion, [know that] I do not worship those whom you worship besides Allah. Rather, I worship Allah who takes your souls, and its validity (صحّته), then I do not worship those whom you worship besides Allah. Rather, I worship Allah who takes your souls, so He is the One worthy (الحقيق) of being feared, hoped in, and worshipped. And He only specified the mention of taking the souls (التوفّي) for the purpose of warning (التهديد). وَأُمِرْتُ أَنْ أَكُونَ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ** and I have been commanded to be one of the faithful.' —those who affirm (المصدقين) Monotheism (التوحيد). So this is my religion.

(١٠٥) وَأَنْ أَقِمْ وَجْهَكَ لِلدِّينِ حَنِيفاً ** (and set your heart on the religion, as a ḥanīf,) This is grammatically connected (عطف) to أَنْ أَكُونَ ** (that I should be), except that the clause connected to أن is expressed in the imperative form (صيغة الأمر). And the meaning is: I was commanded with uprightness (الاستقامة) and soundness (السّداد) in the religion, by performing the obligatory duties (الفرائض) and refraining from abominations (القبائح). وَلا تَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ. (and do not be one of the polytheists.)

(١٠٦) وَلا تَدْعُ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ ما لا يَنْفَعُكَ ** (106) And do not invoke, besides Allah, what neither benefits you if you call upon it, وَلا يَضُرُّكَ nor harms you if you abandon it. فَإِنْ فَعَلْتَ For if you do that (for if you call upon it), فَإِنَّكَ إِذاً مِنَ الظَّالِمِينَ then you will indeed be among the wrongdoers. For indeed, polytheism (الشّرك) is a great injustice (ظلم عظيم). Al-Qummi (said that this is) an address to the Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم, but the intended meaning is for the people.

(١٠٧) وَإِنْ يَمْسَسْكَ اللهُ بِضُرٍّ ** (107) And if Allah touches you with a distress, And if He afflicts you with it فَلا كاشِفَ لَهُ ** (then there is none who can remove it) who can repel it إِلَّا هُوَ ** (except Him.) except Allah وَإِنْ يُرِدْكَ بِخَيْرٍ فَلا رَادَّ ** (And if He desires any good for you, there is none who can avert) then there is none who can repel **لِفَضْلِهِ ** (His grace.) which He desired for you.

It was said: The mention of desire (الإرادة) with good (الخير) and the mention of touching (المسّ) with harm (الضّرّ), despite the two matters being inseparable (تلازم الأمرين), is to draw attention to the fact that good is desired essentially (بالذات), and that harm only touches them not by the primary intention (القصد الأوّل). And the placement of grace (الفضل) in place of the pronoun is to indicate that He is bestowing grace (متفضّل) with the good He desires for them, and that they have no right (استحقاق) over Him concerning it. And He did not make an exception (in the second clause) because what Allah desires cannot be repelled. He afflicts with it (the good) مَنْ يَشاءُ مِنْ عِبادِهِ وَهُوَ الْغَفُورُ الرَّحِيمُ ** (He afflicts with it whomsoever of His servants He wishes, and He is the All-forgiving, the All-merciful.) So expose yourselves to His mercy through obedience (الطاعة) and do not despair of His forgiveness due to disobedience (المعصية).

(108) قُلْ يا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ قَدْ جاءَكُمُ الْحَقُ ** (108) Say, 'O mankind! The truth has certainly come to you from your Lord.' from your Lord, and no excuse remains for you. فَمَنِ اهْتَدى ** chooses guidance through faith and obedience فَإِنَّما يَهْتَدِي لِنَفْسِهِ ** because its benefit is for his own soul. وَمَنْ ضَلَ ** chooses error through denial (الجحود) فَإِنَّما يَضِلُّ عَلَيْها ** because its consequence (وبال) is upon his own soul. وَما أَنَا عَلَيْكُمْ بِوَكِيلٍ ** as a guardian (حفيظ) to whom your affair is entrusted, or to compel you to what I desire. I am only a bearer of good news (بشير) and a warner (نذير).

(١٠٩) وَاتَّبِعْ ما يُوحى إِلَيْكَ ** (109) And follow what is revealed to you by compliance (بالامتثال) and conveyance (والتبليغ). وَاصْبِرْ ** (109) and be patient regarding their call (دعوتهم) and enduring their harm (واحتمال أذاهم). حَتَّى يَحْكُمَ اللهُ ** (109) until Allah judges in your favor (لَكَ) with victory over them and dominance (والغلبة). وَهُوَ خَيْرُ الْحاكِمِينَ ** (109) and He is the best of judges. because He judges only with truth (بالحقّ) and justice (والعدل).

في ثواب الأعمال In Thawab al-A'mal, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Whoever recites Surah Yunus every two or three months, it is not feared for him that he will be among the ignorant (الْجَاهِلِينَ), and on the Day of Resurrection, he will be among the near ones (الْمُقَرَّبِينَ), if Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, wills.

footnote (١) And it is the Quran, the religion of Islam, and the proofs (*الأدلة*) indicating its validity. And it was said that by *الحقّ* (the Truth) He means the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and his manifest miracles (*ومعجزاته الظّاهرة*). (m.n.)
